item.title: Associate Professor in Political Science
item.description: The Department of Political Science at Aarhus BSS, Aarhus University, is seeking dedicated and ambitious scholars to join our dynamic academic environment. As one of Europe’s largest and highest‑ranked political science departments, we offer excellent opportunities to develop your research profile and contribute to high‑quality teaching. We now invite applications for one or more permanent associate professor positions in Political Science, starting January 2027 or as soon as possible thereafter subject to mutual agreement. Job description We are hiring within the fields of one or more of these subjects: Comparative Politics International Relations Political Behaviour Political Sociology Political Theory Public Administration Public Policy The successful applicant is expected to contribute significantly to the department’s research and teaching environment and activities. We expect you to teach and supervise students at Bachelor’s and Master’s level and to carry out individual and group-based research of the highest international standard. We expect active participation in the activities of the department and the relevant sub section. Your qualifications Applicants must possess research qualifications and teaching skills corresponding to those obtained after successful completion of a period of employment as an assistant professor, but equivalent academic qualifications may also be accepted, i.e. applicants ideally have typically 3-8 years of teaching and research experience after completion of their PhD. It is required that non-Danish speakers learn Danish and will be able to teach and conduct examinations in Danish within a short period (3 years at the most). If necessary, the department will grant time off from teaching to attend Danish courses during the first two years of employment. Permanent employment as associate professor at Aarhus BSS requires completion of the supervision course and also the course Online and blended learning provided by Centre for Educational Development at Aarhus University. If you have not completed the courses before commencement of employment, continued employment will be conditional on completion of the courses within a two-year period. The required qualifications are elaborated below: Research qualifications Applicants are expected to have an international research profile with an emphasis on original research of high quality and show progress and momentum in research activities. The department is particularly looking for applicants with the potential of defining the future research agenda within their field. This potential must be documented through a significant body of high-quality research. To assess research qualifications, the assessment and hiring committee will consider the following parameters: Progression in research. The applicant must also demonstrate progression in the form of research publications that go beyond the PhD dissertation thematically, theoretically, methodologically or empirically. Emphasis will be put on recent research, and the evaluation of the applicant’s research will take number of years at current academic level into account. Periods of leave will be observed in the assessment of productivity. Quality of research. The assessment will focus on the scientific originality and contributions of the publications submitted for assessment regardless of outlet. Publication in top political science or top field journals carries weight in this assessment and so does publication of monographs with internationally recognized publishers. However, assessed quality of research carries the most weight and research metrics will only be used in relation to careful assessment of the submitted work. Independence in research. The applicant’s capacity to carry out independent research is also an important assessment criterion and can be documented through, for instance, single-authored publications, coauthor statements declaring the applicant’s contributions, diverse co-authorship, or being PI on research projects. Co-authored publications must be accompanied by co-authorship declarations allowing for an assessment of the independent contribution of the applicant. Didactical/pedagogical competences and experiences The Department will prioritize candidates who demonstrate talent and ability to perform independent, high-quality teaching in subjects that contribute to the Department’s teaching portfolio. Applicants are expected to have substantial teaching experience: a) minimum five courses in total (possibly including teaching tasks as PhD student) and preferably experience with different types of teaching, and b) have completed a pedagogical course in university teaching. Applicants must submit a teaching portfolio clarifying their teaching experience and approach. To assess teaching qualifications, the assessment and hiring committees will consider the following parameters: Breadth of teaching, i.e., a) level of teaching (BA, MA, PhD), b) teaching formats (e.g., TA, seminars, lectures, supervision), or c) subject of teaching relating to diversity in subject matters contributing to the Department’s teaching portfolio. b. Responsibility in teaching, i.e., a) course development and organization (planning syllabus and teaching program), b) exam (formulating exam questions, grading and feedback), or c) course coordination (TA management, co-teaching organization). c. Generally positive teaching evaluations. Who we are With around 120 academic staff members and 50 PhD students, the Department of Political Science is among Europe’s largest political science departments. The research at the department covers most political science disciplines, including public administration, and represents a variety of methodological approaches. We are among Europe’s highest-ranked departments, and our broad research profile enables us to focus on societal problems as they appear in the national and international political arena. This allows us to contribute actively to the development of the discipline. We offer a lively and ambitious research and teaching environment characterized by pluralism and openness. We expect active participation in the activities of the department, which includes being present at the department at least three days per week. For more information about the Department of Political Science, please see here . The Department of Political Science is part of Aarhus BSS , Aarhus University – a top 100 university. Aarhus BSS has achieved the triple-crown AACSB, AMBA and EQUIS accreditations. Further information For further information about the position and the department, please contact Head of Department, Christoffer Green-Pedersen, tel.+45 8716 5692, email: cgp@ps.au.dk . If you need help uploading your application or have any questions about the recruitment process, please contact HR Coordinator Astrid Valdgård Schmidt, email: avs@au.dk . Place of work Department of Political Science, Bartholins Allé 7, DK - 8000 Aarhus. International applicant? Aarhus University offers a broad variety of services for international researchers and accompanying families, including relocation service and career counselling to expat partners. Please find more information here: https://internationalstaff.au.dk/relocationservice/ Please find more information about research opportunities at Aarhus University here: http://international.au.dk/research/ Terms of employment The appointment is made in accordance with the Memorandum on Job Structure for Academic Staff at Danish Universities as well as the circular on the Collective Agreement for Academics Employed by the State (in Danish). The position is at the level of Assistant Professor, with salary determined in accordance with the collective agreement. The job content and qualification requirements are described in further detail in the Ministerial Order on the Appointment of Academic Staff at Universities . Application procedure When you apply for this position, it is mandatory to attach the following: Application Curriculum Vitae. You are encouraged to declare any periods of leave without research activity, including, for example, maternity leave, since your research activities are assessed in relation to your actual research time Education (diploma for master's, PhD and possibly higher doctoral degree) List of publications (the enclosed publications must be clearly marked on the list of publications) Publications. Up to five publications can be included in the application. In the event of several authors the publications must be accompanied by a co-author statement concerning the applicant's share of the collaborative work with the consent of the co-authors. This template may be used for the purpose Teaching portfolio. The specific requirements regarding the documentation can be found here Materials which cannot be uploaded together with the application may be submitted in five copies to Aarhus BSS HR & PhD, Aarhus University, Bartholins Allé 16, DK-8000 Aarhus C. Read more about how to apply for an academic post at Aarhus BSS here The evaluation process Shortlisting is applied. This means that an appointment committee (assisted by the chair of the assessment committee) advises the head of department on which applicants possess the qualifications, competencies, experience and potential described above under “Your qualifications”, notably in fields with specific departmental research and teaching needs and in accordance with the faculty’s criteria and guidelines for evaluating candidates to permanent positions. Through the shortlisting process, the head of department selects the applicants who will receive a thorough assessment of their professional qualifications. The applicants will then be assessed by an expert assessment committee. All applicants will be notified whether or not their application has been shortlisted and forwarded to the assessment committee for evaluation. Shortlisted applicants will be informed about the compositi
item.dc:creator: Aarhus University

item.title: Assistant Professor in Political Science
item.description: The Department of Political Science at Aarhus BSS, Aarhus University, is seeking ambitious and dedicated scholars to join one of Europe’s leading political science environments, offering excellent opportunities to develop an international research profile and contribute to high-quality teaching. We invite applications for one or more positions as Assistant Professor in Political Science. The assistant professorship is a full-time fixed-term position for up to four years starting in January 2027 or as soon as possible hereafter subject to mutual agreement. Job description The successful applicant is expected to contribute significantly to the department’s research and teaching environment and to the overall research strategy of the department. We are hiring within the fields of one or more of these subjects: Comparative Politics International Relations Political Behaviour Political Sociology Political Theory Public Administration Public Policy We expect you to teach and supervise students at Bachelor’s and Master’s levels and to carry out individual and group-based research of the highest international standard. We expect active participation in the activities of the department and the relevant subsection. Your qualifications Relevant PhD. Applicants are formally deemed qualified if they hold a PhD in political science or related fields. If the degree is not obtained yet, the applicant may start as research assistant and advance to an assistant professor position when the degree is granted. Applicants should have obtained their PhD within the past 0–3 years, and only in rare cases more than 3 years ago. Teaching qualifications. The applicant documents teaching experience and positive evaluations of the teaching. The Department will prioritize candidates who demonstrate talent and ability to perform independent, high-quality teaching in subjects that contribute to the Department’s teaching portfolio. Research qualifications. The applicant documents the ability to write high-quality research products publishable in outlets relevant for political science. The Department prioritizes applicants who demonstrate the talent and ability to produce solid and original research results and who are believed to be able to contribute to the further development of the Department’s research portfolio. To assess such talent and ability, publication in top political science or top field journals or with internationally recognized publishers is one element, however, the assessment panel’s evaluation of research quality carries the greatest weight, and the Department also welcomes applications from early-career scholars without publications but with high-quality working papers. Who we are With around 120 academic staff members and 50 PhD students, the Department of Political Science is among Europe’s largest political science departments. The research at the department covers most political science disciplines, including public administration, and represents a variety of methodological approaches. We are among Europe’s highest-ranked departments, and our broad research profile enables us to focus on societal problems as they appear in the national and international political arena. This allows us to contribute actively to the development of the discipline. We offer a lively and ambitious research and teaching environment characterized by pluralism and openness. We expect active participation in the activities of the department, which includes being present at the department at least three days per week. For more information about the Department of Political Science, please see here . The Department of Political Science is part of Aarhus BSS , Aarhus University – a top 100 university. Aarhus BSS has achieved the triple-crown AACSB, AMBA and EQUIS accreditations. Further information For further information about the position and the department, please contact Head of Department, Professor Christoffer Green-Pedersen tel.: +45 87 16 56 92, email: cgp@ps.au.dk . If you need help uploading your application or have any questions about the recruitment process, please contact HR Coordinator Astrid Valdgård Schmidt, email: avs@au.dk . Place of work Department of Political Science, Bartholins Alle 7, DK-8000 Aarhus C. International applicant? Aarhus University offers a broad variety of services for international researchers and accompanying families, including relocation service and career counselling to expat partners. Please find more information here: internationalstaff.au.dk/relocationservice Please find more information about research opportunities at Aarhus University here: international.au.dk/research Terms of employment The appointment is made in accordance with the Memorandum on Job Structure for Academic Staff at Danish Universities as well as the circular on the Collective Agreement for Academics Employed by the State (in Danish). The position is at the level of Assistant Professor, with salary determined in accordance with the collective agreement. The job content and qualification requirements are described in further detail in the Ministerial Order on the Appointment of Academic Staff at Universities . Application procedure When you apply for this position it is mandatory to attach the following: Application Curriculum Vitae. You are encouraged to declare any periods of leave without research activity, including, for example, maternity leave, since your research activities are assessed in relation to your actual research time Education (diploma for master's, PhD and possibly higher doctoral degree) List of publications (the enclosed publications must be clearly marked on the list of publications) Publications. Up to three publications may be submitted. In the event of several authors the publications must be accompanied by a co-author statement concerning the applicant's share of the collaborative work with the consent of the co-authors. This template may be used for the purpose Teaching portfolio. The specific requirements regarding the documentation can be found here Read more about how to apply for an academic position at Aarhus BSS here . The evaluation process Shortlisting is applied. This means that an appointment committee (assisted by the chair of the assessment committee) advises the head of department on which applicants possess the qualifications, competencies, experience and potential described above under “Your qualifications”, notably in fields with specific departmental research and teaching needs. Through the shortlisting process, the head of department selects the applicants who will receive a thorough assessment of their professional qualifications. The applicants will then be assessed by an expert assessment committee. All applicants will be notified whether or not their application has been shortlisted and forwarded to the assessment committee for evaluation. Shortlisted applicants will be informed about the composition of the committee. Please note that the assessment of applicants is based solely on the material received prior to the application deadline. Read more about the recruitment process, shortlisting procedure and guidelines for assessment committees here . The application must be submitted via Aarhus University’s recruitment system, which can be accessed under the job advertisement on Aarhus University's website . Aarhus University’s ambition is to be an attractive and inspiring workplace for all and to foster a culture in which each individual has opportunities to thrive, achieve and develop. We view equality and diversity as assets, and we welcome all applicants. Aarhus University Aarhus University is an academically diverse and research-intensive university with a strong commitment to high-quality research and education and the development of society nationally and globally. The university offers an inspiring research and teaching environment to its 37,000 students (FTEs) and 8,700 employees and has an annual revenue of EUR 1.106 billion. Learn more at www.international.au.dk
item.dc:creator: Aarhus University

item.title: Senior Research Fellow in Responsible AI Translation
item.description: Full Time Fixed Term until 31/03/2028 Applications are invited for a Research Fellow position with Responsible AI UK (RAi UK), based at the University of Southampton. The post is full-time for 20 months, with potential for extension. The post holder will not be required to be based at Southampton but must be able to meet in person with the team once a week. You will join Responsible AI UK , a £31 million UKRI-funded national programme spanning 28 UK universities and over 150 partner organisations across academia, industry, government and civil society. RAi UK is one of the largest responsible AI research programmes in the UK, working at the interface of interdisciplinary research, policy and practice. You will work alongside the CEO, Professor Sarvapali (Gopal) Ramchurn, and the RAi UK leadership team. About the role This is an opportunity for an experienced postdoctoral researcher to work at the heart of one of the UK’s largest responsible AI programmes. The post holder will translate technical outputs from across the RAi UK portfolio and the wider Responsible AI landscape into clear, evidence-based policy advice for government, industry and the public. The research will focus on: Agentic AI systems and their governance Physical AI and its integration with real-world systems Measurement and evaluation of agentic AI performance The implications of these technologies for government, industry and the wider public The post holder will design and convene roundtables, workshops and events with senior stakeholders across UK government, regulators, industry, civil society and international partners. They will synthesise findings into white papers, policy briefings and consultation responses, and will represent RAi UK at national and international policy fora. The role may require travel across the UK and internationally. To be successful you will have: A PhD (or equivalent professional experience) in a discipline relevant to responsible AI, including computer science, AI, data science, science and technology studies, law, public policy, ethics, sociology, or political science Demonstrable experience translating technical research into policy-relevant outputs such as white papers, briefings, consultation responses or evidence submissions A strong understanding of contemporary debates in AI governance, evaluation, assurance, agentic AI and/or physical AI Experience designing and convening events or roundtables with senior stakeholders from government, industry and/or civil society Excellent written and oral communication skills, with the ability to present complex concepts clearly to diverse audiences Willingness and ability to travel across the UK and internationally to represent RAi UK This post offers significant exposure to senior policymakers, opportunities for visible thought leadership, and the chance to shape national and international conversations on the responsible governance of agentic and physical AI. For further information about Responsible AI UK, please visit: https://rai.ac.uk Further details: This role does not qualify for Skilled Worker Visa sponsorship. To explore alternative UK visa options, see gov.uk We are committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and welcome applicants who support our mission of inclusivity. The University is a signatory of the Technician Commitment and a partner affiliate of the National Technician Development Centre (NTDC). We are committed to supporting our technical staff and offer a wide range of opportunities including networking, mentoring, training, professional registration and career development. Apply by 11:59pm UK time on the closing date. For assistance contact Recruitment on +44(0)2380592750 or recruitment@soton.ac.uk quoting the job number.
item.dc:creator: University of Southampton

item.title: Public Affairs Manager
item.description: Community Pharmacy England is looking for a Public Affairs Manager to help as it works to influence primary care and wider healthcare policies. Community Pharmacy England represents the 10,400+ NHS community pharmacies in England. These pharmacies are at the heart of primary care, ensuring that millions of people receive vital prescription medicines safely and efficiently and offering healthcare advice and support to the public. In recent years pharmacies have delivered a range of public health and clinical services including vaccinations, Pharmacy First, and advice on using medicines. But the network of community pharmacies is under enormous pressure, with a £2billion funding gap combined with serious operational pressures. Community Pharmacy England’s focus is on securing a sustainable future for community pharmacies: negotiating the contractual framework with the NHS and Government, and providing leadership, representation and support for pharmacies. Strategic influencing is at the heart of this work and we deliver a wide public affairs work programme to support this. This will continue to be critical as community pharmacies battle sustained pressures, despite their excellent performance and ambitious aspirations for the future. We are looking for a Public Affairs Manager to join our small, busy team and help to take this work forwards, initially on a maternity cover basis. The ideal candidate will already have a great contacts-book across Westminster as well as a proven track-record in successfully raising profile and influencing Government policy. We are looking for someone who is comfortable dealing with senior stakeholders and advising on policy objectives as well as devising plans to bring about change. An assured self-starter, you’ll be excited to roll your sleeves up as part of small and busy team working within a complex matrix of sector stakeholders. You’ll be highly competent at drafting persuasive briefings for MPs and other audiences, as well as acting as an external spokesperson. An understanding of healthcare commissioning in England would be a great advantage. Full details can be found on our website – https://cpe.org.uk/our-news/were-hiring-join-our-public-affairs-and-influencing-team/ Closing Date : 20 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates We may interview candidates before the deadline of 20 July 2026. Application Details To apply, please send a CV and covering letter to shine.brownsell@cpe.org.uk. For any informal enquiries please email shine.brownsell@cpe.org.uk. Website : http://cpe.org.uk/
item.dc:creator: Community Pharmacy England

item.dc:creator: ifG

item.title: Caseworker
item.description: Tessa Munt is the MP for the Wells and Mendip Hills constituency. This is an exciting opportunity as she recruits a Caseworker to join her team and assist her constituents with the matters which concern them. In this office based role, you can work full or part time; job sharing welcome. Full time is 37.5 hrs a week. Roles have 3 months’ probation with a view to a long-term position. Responsibilities • Receiving high volumes of enquiries from constituents by phone, letter, email, social media and in meetings; • Dealing with enquiries promptly, sensitively, confidentially; documenting all exchanges, actions and decisions accurately on our casework system in line with GDPR; • Liaising with other team members on progress, trends and observations, drafting responses, collating evidence, preparing reports; updating constituents on progress and follow-up plans; • Building and maintaining good working relationships with government agencies, housing providers, the third sector, local councils and others, aiming to resolve constituents’ concerns; • Organising meetings; note taking at surgeries with Tessa on a rota with others. Skills Required • Strong interpersonal skills; empathy; emotional intelligence; well-developed listening skills; a sensitive but tenacious approach; • Ability to be flexible in a fast-moving environment; • Respect for the confidential nature of working in a sensitive and public-facing role; • Able to work individually and as part of a team; • Excellent organisational and communication skills - written and verbal, with an attention to detail; • Policy research focusing on resolving casework; • Willingness to attend training on policy, law and legislation; • Good knowledge of office based IT systems and admin tasks. Applications from those with experience of advocacy and other public-facing or service roles are welcomed. Caseworkers occasionally travel for work reasons and join Tessa for surgeries and home visits outside usual weekday office hours. Access to the use of a car and a clean driving licence in this rural area is necessary. You don’t have to be a member of the Liberal Democrats to work with Tessa, but empathy with the Party’s aims and values is important. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 17 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will take place during August near Cheddar, Somerset, with a starting date of the 1st September. Application Details Please send a letter (one side of A4) and a CV (on no more than two sides of A4) to tessa.munt.mp@parliament.uk before midnight on closing date. Please put 'Caseworker Application - [Your Name]' in the subject line. Please also let me know if you require any adjustments to meet your needs during the recruitment process. Website : http://www.tessamunt.co.uk
item.dc:creator: Tessa Munt MP (Wells and Mendip Hills)

item.title: Executive Assistant
item.description: Executive Assistant (Part-Time – 3 Days per Week) Location: Constituency office (Newbury) Salary: In line with IPSA pay scales (pro rata) Hours: Part-time (3 days per week, flexibly agreed) An exciting opportunity for a highly organised and detail-focused Executive Assistant to support a busy constituency office. This role is central to ensuring effective administration, financial management, and coordination of the MP’s constituency activities. This position would suit someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, has excellent organisational skills,& takes pride in maintaining accurate records and systems. Key Responsibilities • Provide administrative support to ensure the smooth day-to-day running of the office • Manage the MP’s diary and inbox, including triaging correspondence & allocating casework • Coordinate visits, meetings, and events, including logistics and briefing support; accompany the MP where required • Oversee office administration, systems, and governance processes, ensuring deadlines and priorities are tracked • Lead on IPSA administration and budgeting, including processing invoices, expenses, & maintaining records • Manage suppliers, contracts, & facilities, including liaison with service providers • Coordinate recruitment, onboarding & staff administration, including HR records • Liaise with stakeholders, including local authorities, community organisations, and businesses • Support communications, constituency activity, & provide ad hoc admin support Person Specification • Excellent organisational skills with strong attention to detail • Proven ability to manage competing priorities and meet deadlines • Experience in administration, finance, or office management • Ability to work independently & proactively • Strong communication and interpersonal skills • Discretion in handling sensitive information • Experience with IPSA processes (desirable, not essential) • Interest in politics and public service (desirable) • Ability to work independently and as part of a small team • A proactive and flexible approach to supporting colleagues Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 24 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Application Details Please send a tailored CV (max two pages) and covering letter (max one page) explaining your interest in working for Lee Dillon MP to lee.dillon.mp@parliament.uk. Use the subject line: Executive Assistant: NAME. Due to high application volumes, we’re unable to contact all applicants. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to complete a task, with a small number progressing to interview. You will only be contacted if you reach the next stage. If you have not heard by 10 August, please assume your application has been unsuccessful. Website : http://www.leedillon.co.uk
item.dc:creator: Lee Dillon, MP (Newbury)

item.title: Parliamentary Researcher
item.description: This position will involve parliamentary research in the Parliamentary Office of Dr Roz Savage MP. This position would suit a candidate who has some expertise on environmental issues, and who is knowledgeable on campaigns and campaign groups in the sector, as well as a general interest in politics. We are looking for a candidate who is hardworking, enthusiastic, and shares Roz’s passion for community, rural affairs and nature. Key responsibilities Analyse, evaluate and interpret data to ensure Member is accurately informed on key issues Assist in writing external communications including speeches for the MP Assist with casework as required Attend and participate in meetings as appropriate Compilation of briefing packs for MPs, take notes of meetings and draft appropriate submissions Develop and maintain current knowledge on relevant areas of parliamentary business including Motions, legislation, Hansard, debates etc. This would include issues of importance both nationally and in the local constituency. Diary Management Engage confidentially, professionally, with care and integrity with members of the public, through telephone enquiries, social media, email and face to face Give advice on policy issues Have a keen understanding of environmental issues, particularly on topics like food, farming, and nature policy Have working knowledge of relevant legislation and upcoming business including how they impact on the constituency Help draft amendments for reports and prepare briefing material Leverage AI tools responsibly to improve productivity, decision-making, and problem-solving while ensuring ethical and informed use. Prepare and present briefing notes for committees, press releases, parliamentary questions etc. Project Management Project Work Research issues which face the constituency and Roz’s wider environmental campaigns Undertake research on relevant subjects as directed Work with colleagues in communications/casework to identify potential impacts from the research work and disseminate them effectively This is a London based role, applicants must be able to work from Parliament There may be a requirement for the role holder to work outside of normal working hours and travel for work occasionally Closing Date : 16 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Closing Date: 16th July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Successful candidates will be interviewed over Teams on the week commencing 20th July. The start date will be 1st September 2026. Application Details A CV and cover letter to be submitted to roz.savage.mp@parliament.uk Your cover letter should highlight how your experience matches the job description and why you are looking to work for Dr Roz Savage. Website : https://www.rozsavage.uk/

item.title: FTI Consulting PR & Communications Trainee
item.description: Are you a recent graduate eager to start a career in Public Relations, Communications or Public Affairs? Our graduate Public Relations & Communications Training Programme is a paid 8-week traineeship that aims to provide a practical yet immersive opportunity to start a career in PR. Delivered by FTI Consulting's Strategic Communications practice, the FTI Consulting PR & Communications Training Programme will run from Monday, October 5th – Friday, November 27th, 2026. What does the programme involve? Weekly in-person masterclasses delivered by a range of PR & Communications agencies & in-house communications teams to showcase and help you decide the area of communications you would love to go into. Immersive in-person workshops delivered by FTI Consulting that provide an insight into their various practices. This can include areas such as ESG, Public Affairs, Crisis Communications, Corporate Reputation & Financial Communications Practical training on the role of a Communications Consultant alongside building a generalist skill set to confidently deliver the fundamentals. Some training will include; understanding the campaign creation process, learning how to present, strategy, press release writing, media monitoring and stakeholder engagement. 1-2-1 mentoring from FTI Consulting team members, weekly check-ins, CV & cover letter workshops, mock interviews and job applications support. Programme Eligibility You must be from a Black, Asian and/ or minority ethnic background. You must be a graduate who has graduated within the last three years (or a final year student that will be finished with their studies by the time of the programme). We accept applications from graduates who have studied any subject. You must be a British national or have the right to work in the UK by commencement of employment. You must be strongly interested in the work of FTI Consulting’s strategic communications practice. You must also demonstrate an interest in pursuing a career in Public Relations (PR) and communications. You must be able to commit to the full in-person programme, which will be based in London (Monday-Friday, 9.30am-5.30pm) Closing Date : 7 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates If successful, applicants will be invited to attend a final stage in-person assessment day on Friday September 11th, 2026 . Start Date : Monday October 5th - November 27th 2026 Application Details To apply for the programme, please click the application link below to complete our written application form. Application Link : https://www.taylorbennettfoundation.org/fti-consulting-pr-training-programme/ If you have any queries regarding the PR & Commnuications Training Programme or application process, please contact applications@taylorbennettfoundation.org. Website : https://www.taylorbennettfoundation.org/
item.dc:creator: Taylor Bennett Foundation & FTI Consulting

item.title: Research Fellow in Responsible AI Translation
item.description: Full Time Fixed Term until 31/03/2028 Applications are invited for a Research Fellow position with Responsible AI UK (RAi UK), based at the University of Southampton. The post is full-time for 20 months, with potential for extension. The post holder will not be required to be based at Southampton but must be able to meet in person with the team once a week. You will join Responsible AI UK , a £31 million UKRI-funded national programme spanning 28 UK universities and over 150 partner organisations across academia, industry, government and civil society. RAi UK is one of the largest responsible AI research programmes in the UK, working at the interface of interdisciplinary research, policy and practice. You will work alongside the CEO, Professor Sarvapali (Gopal) Ramchurn, and the RAi UK leadership team. About the role This is an opportunity for an experienced postdoctoral researcher to work at the heart of one of the UK’s largest responsible AI programmes. The post holder will translate technical outputs from across the RAi UK portfolio and the wider Responsible AI landscape into clear, evidence-based policy advice for government, industry and the public. The research will focus on: Agentic AI systems and their governance Physical AI and its integration with real-world systems Measurement and evaluation of agentic AI performance The implications of these technologies for government, industry and the wider public The post holder will design and convene roundtables, workshops and events with senior stakeholders across UK government, regulators, industry, civil society and international partners. They will synthesise findings into white papers, policy briefings and consultation responses, and will represent RAi UK at national and international policy fora. The role may require travel across the UK and internationally. To be successful you will have: A PhD (or equivalent professional experience) in a discipline relevant to responsible AI, including computer science, AI, data science, science and technology studies, law, public policy, ethics, sociology, or political science Demonstrable experience translating technical research into policy-relevant outputs such as white papers, briefings, consultation responses or evidence submissions A strong understanding of contemporary debates in AI governance, evaluation, assurance, agentic AI and/or physical AI Experience designing and convening events or roundtables with senior stakeholders from government, industry and/or civil society Excellent written and oral communication skills, with the ability to present complex concepts clearly to diverse audiences Willingness and ability to travel across the UK and internationally to represent RAi UK This post offers significant exposure to senior policymakers, opportunities for visible thought leadership, and the chance to shape national and international conversations on the responsible governance of agentic and physical AI. For further information about Responsible AI UK, please visit: https://rai.ac.uk Further details: This role does not qualify for Skilled Worker Visa sponsorship. To explore alternative UK visa options, see gov.uk We are committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and welcome applicants who support our mission of inclusivity. The University is a signatory of the Technician Commitment and a partner affiliate of the National Technician Development Centre (NTDC). We are committed to supporting our technical staff and offer a wide range of opportunities including networking, mentoring, training, professional registration and career development. Apply by 11:59pm UK time on the closing date. For assistance contact Recruitment on +44(0)2380592750 or recruitment@soton.ac.uk quoting the job number.
item.dc:creator: University of Southampton

item.title: British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowship Opportunities
item.description: The College of Arts and Law at the University of Birmingham encourages outstanding postdoctoral researchers to apply to the British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowship, for Fellowships hosted at Birmingham starting in the 2027/28 academic year. These three-year fellowships are intended to enable scholars in the arts and humanities and social sciences to complete a major piece of publishable research and to establish their profile within their field and across the wider academic community. Ranked 76 th in the QS Rankings for Arts and Humanities, the College of Arts and Law is home to a thriving research culture across five Schools: Birmingham Law School; English, Drama and Creative Studies; History and Cultures; Languages, Cultures, Art History and Music; and Philosophy, Theology and Religion. Research facilities include our outstanding collections and libraries, the Barber Institute of Fine Art, and eye tracking, motion capture, digital media, VR, AR, data processing, and corpus linguistics laboratories dedicated to arts and humanities researchers. Further information about the College can be found here: https://www.birmingham.ac.uk/research/artslaw . Applications are welcomed from colleagues with strong research proposals who are eligible for the Fellowship because they: Have completed their Viva Voce examination between 1st April 2024 to 1st April 2027 Are a UK/EEA national or completed their doctorate at a UK university or have a strong prior association with the UK academic community Have not yet held a permanent academic appointment Have not previously applied for the BA Postdoctoral Fellowship. The College of Arts and Law will select prospective applicants for the Fellowship based on the quality of the proposal and fit within the College. To enable this selection processes, candidates must send the following information by email to Samantha Taylor, Research Support Officer for the College of Arts and Law ( s.l.taylor.1@bham.ac.uk ) by 5pm on Monday 20th July 2026 ( there is no leeway/margin to go over on page number or word count) : A description of their proposed project ( max . 2 A4 pages, limited informative images and brief references can be on a third page but are not required) A copy of their CV ( max . 2 A4 pages) A summary of why the University of Birmingham and the relevant School are a good fit for the project ( max . 300 words) In the body of the email: The name of their proposed mentor and confirmation they have agreed to act as mentor for the application and fellowship, please also cc the mentor to the email The name of their proposed referee (this cannot be the mentor, the supervisor or the internal examiner) The date of the Viva Voce Confirmation that they have not yet held a permanent academic role AND that they have not previously applied for a BA Postdoctoral Fellowship That they are either a UK/EEA national or did their PhD in a UK university Any exemptions/clarifications from the above (including if eligibility is based on ‘strong prior association’) need to be confirmed by the applicant with the British Academy and the email included or forwarded to us (this can be after the July deadline but ineligible applications or refused exemption will result in the EOI being withdrawn). Incomplete/incorrect expressions of interest will not be considered, please check everything carefully. Selected candidates will be supported by their mentors and the University’s Research Strategy and Services Division to develop their full proposal, which must be submitted to the British Academy by 5pm on 30 th September 2026. Appointments at the University of Birmingham are dependent on the award of the Fellowship. We know that diversity strengthens our research community and actively encourage applicants from diverse career paths and all backgrounds. For more information on the scheme’s aims and eligibility criteria, please click the above 'Apply' button. For general enquiries about the scheme or working at the University of Birmingham, please contact Samantha Taylor at s.l.taylor.1@bham.ac.uk . Closing Date: 20/07/2026 @ 5 pm
item.dc:creator: University of Birmingham

item.title: Research Fellow in Public Engagement and Science Governance
item.description: Are you an experienced researcher with a passion for public engagement and the social dimensions of emerging science? Do you want to help shape new approaches to engaging diverse communities in conversations about genomic science? We are seeking a Research Fellow to join the Wellcome-funded project, Tuning EARS for the Future: Reframing Engagement in (Genomic) Science . This project reframes public engagement from cataloguing stakeholder views to addressing the relational gaps that shape how publics and stakeholders engage with emerging science. Working closely with Professor Joy Y. Zhang and a multidisciplinary research team, you will contribute to the development of innovative public dialogue approaches, collaborate with a range of stakeholders, and help generate research that informs the future of engagement in genomic science. You will be experienced in collaborative research and public engagement with comprehensive knowledge of emerging biomedical or genomic sciences and their associated social, ethical and policy dimensions. The post is full-time and for a fixed period to the end of August 2027. As a Research Fellow you can expect to be involved in : Designing and conducting empirical research to support the successful delivery of the Tuning EARS for the Future project. Conducting in-depth media, policy, and secondary data analysis, synthesizing findings to generate meaningful insights for the project. Co-leading high-impact academic publications that contribute to scholarly discourse and advance knowledge in the field. Playing a central role in curating, publicising and delivering engagement activities with diverse stakeholders. What will you bring to the role? A PhD, or equivalent, in a relevant discipline such as sociology, science communication, anthropology, political science, bioethics or a related field. Experience of independently designing and conducting rigorous empirical social research, with a strong publication record. Knowledge of the social, ethical and policy dimensions of emerging biomedical or genomic sciences. Experience of public engagement or science communication, with excellent collaborative and communication skills. What we can offer in return: As a member of our team, you can expect a friendly, open and collaborative working environment and support in your development and wellbeing. You'll enjoy a range of great staff benefits including: Hybrid working options allowing a flexible blend of office and home working (to be agreed in line with business needs - for this role, remote work may be supported, however there will be a need to attend the Canterbury campus regularly) 43 days' leave per year (personal leave, bank holidays and additional days allocated for the Christmas period, pro rata for part-time staff) Excellent pension scheme with generous employer contributions Corporate employee-funded healthcare plan, in partnership with Benenden Health For more information about what you can look forward to if you join us, visit our dedicated webpage: Working at Kent For enquiries about the roles, please contact Joy Y. Zhang via email at y.zhang-203@kent.ac.uk . For further information regarding the application process, please contact peopleserviceshr@kent.ac.uk quoting reference number SSS-038-26 We particularly welcome applications from female candidates as they are under-represented at this level in this area
item.dc:creator: University of Kent

item.title: Parliamentary Intern (Part-Time – 2 Days per Week)
item.description: Location: Westminster, London Contract: Fixed-term (October – April) Salary: In line with IPSA internship rates Hours: 2 days per week A part-time Parliamentary Intern is sought to support a busy parliamentary office. This role offers an excellent opportunity to gain hands-on experience in Parliament, supporting legislative, research, and communications work. Key Responsibilities Assist with parliamentary research, briefings, and policy work Monitor debates, select committees, and legislative developments Draft correspondence, speeches, and written materials Support preparation for debates, questions, and parliamentary interventions Assist with inbox management and stakeholder engagement Attend meetings, briefings, and events in Westminster where appropriate Provide administrative support to the wider team Support communications work, including drafting content and monitoring media Undertake ad hoc tasks as required to support the parliamentary office Person Specification Strong written and verbal communication skills Excellent research and analytical abilities High level of organisation and attention to detail Interest in UK politics and parliamentary processes Ability to work accurately under pressure Proactive, adaptable, and willing to learn Previous experience in a political, research, or communications environment (desirable but not essential) Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 24 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Shortlisting will begin after the closing date, with a written task followed by interviews (dates TBC). We may appoint before the closing date if a suitable candidate is identified. Start date: ideally mid-October 2026, subject to completion of security checks. Application Details Please send a tailored CV (maximum two pages) and a covering letter (maximum one page), setting out why you would like to apply for this role with Lee Dillon MP, to lee.dillon.mp@parliament.uk . Use the subject line: Parliamentary Intern: NAME Thank you for your interest in this position and for the time and effort taken to apply. Due to the high volume of applications we receive, we regret that we are unable to contact all applicants individually. Website : http://www.leedillon.co.uk

item.title: Senior Research Fellow (Statecraft and Military Sciences)
item.description: Contract: Full-time, fixed term for 2 years, with potential for a further 3 years Start date: 4 January 2027 or earlier if possible Reports to: SST-CCW Centre Director Pembroke College, Oxford, invites applications for the role of Senior Research Fellow to work with the Strategy, Statecraft, & Technology: Changing Character of War Centre (SST:CCW), a research centre within the college. This is an exciting opportunity for a talented researcher to contribute to interdisciplinary scholarship within an internationally recognised research centre. The post-holder will conduct high-level research in the fields of Statecraft and Military Sciences, contribute to SST:CCW’s research outputs, support visiting fellows, and engage with academic and practitioner communities worldwide. The successful candidate will have a doctorate in a related field and strong research skills, with experience in engaging both academic and practitioner audiences. For further details of the role please click the 'Apply' button above. How to Apply Applications should be sent to elizabeth.robson@pmb.ox.ac.uk by 9am on 12 August 2026 and include: A cover letter detailing how your experience meets the selection criteria; A 2,000-word statement outlining current and future research, including what work you envisage doing in the Centre; A detailed CV; The details of two references who will be contacted after the interview stage. Interviews will be held during the week commencing 7 September 2026 and shortlisted candidates will be required to present a summary of their research. For enquiries about the role, please contact Dr Rob Johnson, SST-CCW Director at: robert.johnson@pmb.ox.ac.uk Pembroke College is an equal opportunities employer. It welcomes diversity amongst its staff and students and all reasonable adjustments will be made to the recruitment process, working arrangements and/or environment to accommodate applicants with any form of disability. If you have a disability, please let us know whether you believe there are any reasonable adjustments we should be making to assist you with your application. Please also let us know of any reasonable adjustments that we would need to make for you if you are invited to attend an interview. Pembroke College is dedicated to fostering an environmentally sustainable community. We are striving towards achieving net zero carbon emissions and biodiversity net gain and believe that sustainability is a shared responsibility and integral to all roles within the College. By embedding sustainability into our daily operations and decision-making processes, we aim to create a positive impact on our community as well as the wider world. All data supplied by candidates will be used only for the purpose of determining their suitability for the post, following the principles of the Data Protection Act 2018.

item.title: Full Time Caseworker/Researcher
item.description: We are looking for an experienced caseworker/researcher with excellent research and communication skills. You will need experience of preparing written and verbal briefing materials and the ability to work proactively with a variety of internal and external stakeholders. Good knowledge of parliamentary processes is required; and you should be prepared to be both proactive and flexible, with strong interpersonal and teamworking skills There may be a requirement for the post holder to work outside of the normal working hours and travel to Westminster occasionally Duties and Responsibilities will include: • Manage and progress a portfolio of complex casework, including logging cases, taking appropriate actions promptly, monitoring progress • Liaise with government agencies, council, NHS, voluntary sector and other organizations to help resolve cases • Gather relevant information to assist with resolving cases • Respond to correspondence and enquiries from constituents, including drafting letters, emails and documents • Attend constituency surgeries and meetings • Retain records and information confidentially and in line with GDPR • Maintain, update and develop knowledge of relevant legislation and guidance • Excellent interpersonal and oral and written communication skills, with a positive and flexible approach • Excellent IT skills including a casework tracking system • Ability to draft letters and emails to a high professional standard • Ability to write speeches and press releases • Ability to research across a wide range of issues • Motivated to work independently as well as the ability to work effectively as part of a small team. • Good office administration skills • An interest in current affairs and politics and supportive of the values of the Labour Party Person Specification/Skills: • Previous caseworker experience in an MP's office or in a similar role • Liaising with Government agencies • Have political awareness • Knowledge of devolution • Good command of English • Speech writer ability • Research skills Desirable skills: • Local knowledge of the Newport West & Islwyn Constituency • Understanding of the Caseworker.mp system • Driving licence and use of a car Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 17 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates TBA Application Details Interested applicants should email their CV and a short covering letter detailing how you are suitable for the role to Karen French, Office Manager at karen.french@parliament.uk Website : http://www.ruthjones.wales
item.dc:creator: Ruth Jones MP for Newport West & Islwyn

item.title: Policy Manager
item.description: ERA, the digital entertainment and retail association is a UK trade organisation formed specifically to act as a forum for the digital and physical retail sectors of the music, video and videogames industries. We are looking for an exceptional Policy Manager to join our team working alongside our CEO and Communications and Policy Team. Key functions: • Building and implementing ERA’s Government Affairs Agenda • Building relations across the policy teams within ERA’s membership • Conducting advocacy efforts cross party and at all levels of government and policy making • Providing input into legislative and policy initiatives and strategies related to specific policy objectives, including inter alia: o Artificial Intelligence , digital replicas and related issues o Music Streaming in particular fraud, transparency, metadata and creator remuneration o Supporting high street retail, in particular the Independent Sector • Representing ERA Policy in relation to related organisations such as Creative UK, DiMA and UK Music • Establishing rapport with industry associations and trade organisations to ensure representation of ERA’s interests • Participating in industry and trade groups to develop and advocate for common industry positions • Working with ERA and its members to understand their goals and objectives to ensure that government relations activities are aligned to their businesses • Developing position papers and discussion guides • Defining and implementing a European policy strategy Requirements: • 5 to 6 years plus working within a policy or public affairs focused role • Experience in both developing and delivering a politically focused strategy • Strong Political and Departmental relationships (in particular DCMS, DSIT and IPO) • Ability to draft clear, engaging and persuasive written briefings and responses to consultations • Knowledge of the music, creative industries and the wider entertainment and tech industries • Strong communication skills and the ability to articulate clear arguments in political meetings Closing Date : 31 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will take place July / Aug for an ideal start date in September (flexible) Application Details Please send a CV and covering letter to kim@eraltd.org Website : http://eraltd.org
item.dc:creator: ERA, the digital entertainment and retail association

item.title: Content & Social Media Manager
item.description: This is a new and exciting role at ISBA, helping modernise and strengthen our digital communications. The role will support the growth of ISBA’s profile, reach and relevance by ensuring that our policy, advocacy, thought leadership, events and wider activity are translated into engaging, accessible and platform-appropriate content. This is a specialist role that combines content planning, social media delivery, editorial judgement, multimedia production and digital amplification, helping ensure that ISBA’s communications feel current, high-quality, and consistent with the industry we represent. The postholder will curate, produce and coordinate content, and lead the commissioning of material from third-party creators such as graphic designers. It would strongly suit an entrepreneurial individual who has created and curated digital content for an MP. Key Responsibilities: Leading the delivery of ISBA's social media strategy, helping to increase reach, visibility, consistency of perception, engagement, and relevance across platforms Managing ISBA's day-to-day social media activity Monitoring trends, conversations and opportunities that could improve ISBA's digital relevance, profile and engagement Plan, curate and co-ordinate content that helps package and amplify ISBA's advocacy, policy and insight Produce original content across written, visual, video and audio formats, including short-form and multimedia assets Work with our in-house experts to turn complex issues into accessible content Co-ordinate the development of ISBA’s multimedia output, including planning, recording logistics, editing liaison, promotion and distribution Lead and oversee content commissioned from external creators, including graphic designers, videographers, podcast support and other specialist suppliers as appropriate, ensuring briefs are clear and outputs are onbrand and fit for purpose Develop and deliver digital content that supports ISBA events, campaigns, roundtables, conferences and major external affairs activity before, during and after key moments Create and coordinate live, near-live and post-event content such as social coverage, video snippets, summary materials and other amplification assets Contribute content to the ISBA website and other owned channels in a way that supports both member value and wider external visibility Closing Date : 15 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews expected to take place in the week commencing 20 July. Ideal start date in early September. Application Details For a full Job Description, Person Specification and application details, please email recruit@isba.org.uk with a request for them, with "Content & Social Media Manager" in the subject line. Website : http://www.isba.org.uk

item.title: Public & Regulatory Affairs Manager
item.description: You will help deliver ISBA’s public affairs & regulatory engagement activity, ensuring that our members’ interests are effectively represented with external stakeholders. This is a practical policy, advocacy & engagement role that combines horizon-scanning, policy development, member engagement and external representation. You will help shape ISBA positions on key issues, support parliamentary & regulatory engagement, and contribute to co-ordinated external affairs activity that protects and advances members’ interests. Key Responsibilities Support the delivery of engagement with Westminster, government departments, parliamentarians, devolved administrations & other external stakeholders Monitor political, legislative & regulatory developments affecting advertisers and the wider advertising industry, identifying implications, risks and opportunities for ISBA and our members Draft briefings, consultation responses, speaking notes, policy summaries & other materials that support ISBA’s advocacy and influencing activity Contribute to advocacy campaigns & public affairs activity around agreed policy priorities, ensuring that ISBA’s positions are clear, evidence-based & timely Support ISBA’s engagement with key regulatory and industry bodies, including the Committee of Advertising Practice, the Advertising Standards Authority, and other relevant forums and stakeholders Track developments in advertising, media, privacy, platform and related regulation, and help communicate relevant implications to members and colleagues Support the work of ISBA’s Public Affairs Forum and other relevant member working groups, ensuring they are well-informed, well-organised and useful in surfacing member priorities and intelligence Help develop member-facing policy content, updates, briefings and guidance notes that make complex developments more accessible and actionable Work collaboratively with the wider External Affairs team to ensure that policy and regulatory activity is effectively integrated with PR, communications, content and wider campaign delivery Represent ISBA externally at meetings, industry events and stakeholder forums where appropriate, including organisations such as the Advertising Association and the World Federation of Advertisers where relevant support is needed Closing Date : 17 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Expected Interview Dates: Week commencing 27 July Start Dates: Ideally in early September Application Details For a full Job Description and Person Specification, please email recruit@isba.org.uk with a request for them, with "Public & Regulatory Affairs Manager" in the subject line. Website : http://www.isba.org.uk

item.title: Campaign Manager
item.description: Wokingham has a reputation of being one of the most effective and successful Lib Dem teams in the UK. We made gains in the last six sets of local elections in a row, won the Wokingham Parliamentary seat at the 2024 General Election and took outright control of Wokingham Borough Council in 2025. We are looking for a Campaign Manager for the 2027 and 2028 Wokingham Borough elections, as well as for the next General Election in both Wokingham and the neighbouring Earley and Woodley Parliamentary constituencies. This is a job share, based in Wokingham but employed by the Wokingham Borough party for four days each working week and by the Reading party for one day. You will work with both parties’ local staff and volunteers, Clive Jones, the Liberal Democrat MP for Wokingham and the Parliamentary candidate for Earley and Woodley The job will involve:- Designing and implementing campaign plans for Wokingham and Reading Liberal Democrats. Working on the design, production, printing, management and distribution of campaign literature, both printed and digital. Recruiting and organising local volunteers for campaign activities, matching them with tasks and making them feel valued; assisting generally with communications and logistics with this group. Communicating with members and supporters regarding party activities and events. Member engagement, including phoning members and helping to organise newsletters. Maintaining records and databases to a high standard of accuracy and timeliness, including data entry. Manage the finances and paperwork of the Reading local election campaign. Skills and Experience. We are looking for candidates who are proactive “self starters” and can demonstrate:- Excellent organisational skills. Experience of working with volunteers individually and in large teams. IT literacy and a willingness to learn. A passion for politics and support for the values and aims of the Liberal Democrats. It will be helpful if you have a full UK Driving Licence and access to a vehicle, but this is not a requirement. The role may require you to work outside normal working hours where necessary and to travel for work occasionally. Our interview panel can discuss this with you. Closing Date : 17 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will be scheduled for the week commencing Monday 20th July 2026 We would like the successful candidate to start work in early September 2026, but this is negotiable. Application Details To apply for this role, please submit a covering letter and your CV to Chris Ward at chriswardlibdems@gmail.com Website : http://Wokinghamlibdems.org.uk
item.dc:creator: Wokingham and Reading Liberal Democrats

item.title: Whistleblowing Appropriate Person(s) - Lead and Deputies
item.description: The Appropriate Person will be a member who is removed from the day-to-day running of the Party. They would not, for example, be a member or recent member of the Federal Board, Federal Council, Federal Finance and Resources Committee or Federal Communications and Elections Committee. They could, for example, be a member of the Federal Policy Committee or Federal Conference Committee, but this role will require periods of significant time commitment that may not be compatible with the work of either committee. They may hold other positions in their Local Party or Affiliated Organisation, and might be an elected representative or Peer (although parliamentarians are less likely to have the time to commit to this role). We are looking for people with professional or voluntary experience of policy areas or professional areas related to whistleblowing/protected disclosures, or where there are strong transferable skills. This could include, but is not limited to: Human resources. A legal background. Past experience working for or as a prescribed person. Standards work in local authorities. Legislative or scrutiny experience in the area of whistleblowing/protected disclosure. Experience of casework related to whistleblowing/protected disclosure. Our policy on parental, adoption and dependent leave can be found here. https://www.libdems.org.uk/work-for-us/vacancy/whistleblowing-appropriate-persons-lead-and-deputies W4MP Note: The role being advertised is a voluntary one. As such, there are no set hours and responsibilities and you should be free to come and go as you wish. If the post demands set hours and/or has a specific job description you may be deemed to be a ‘worker’ and be covered by National Minimum Wage/National Living Wage legislation. See http://www.w4mp.org/jobs-listings-events/jobs/ advertising-jobs-with-w4mp/volunteers/what-is-the-national-minimum-wage/ for more information about National Minimum Wage/National Living Wage. Closing Date : 13 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Suitable, shortlisted candidates will be invited to a virtual interview w/c 20th July. The interview panel will be led by the Chair of FPDC. Application Details For questions and further details, and to submit an application, please get in touch with the Company Secretary, Rachel Minshull, at: companysecretary@libdems.org.uk Please apply by email, including: A covering letter of no more than one page of A4 which outlines your suitability for the role. A CV. A completed due diligence form. A completed diversity monitoring form. Applicants are encouraged to inform us if any reasonable adjustments are needed at any stage of the recruitment process. Applications will close at 12 noon Website : https://www.libdems.org.uk/

item.title: Senior Associate
item.description: https://ats.rippling.com/quadrant-strategies/jobs/dcce551a-eb6d-4bbe-8288-a4c22012cb50 RESPONSIBILITIES Contributing to the full research lifecycle, from proposal writing and study design through to analysis, reporting and client presentations Drafting first drafts of clear, well-structured memos, reports and decks that translate research findings into actionable insight for senior audiences Working across qualitative and quantitative methodologies, such as proprietary polling, in-depth interviews, focus groups and other research methodologies Supporting and eventually leading client relationships, communicating findings and recommendations with confidence Collaborating with colleagues across our global offices on shared projects and business development Mentoring more junior colleagues as you grow into the role IDEAL QUALIFICATIONS Strong writing and communication skills - you can make complex ideas clear and compelling for audiences who may not share your expertise Comfort with both qualitative and quantitative research, you do not need to be a specialist in either, but you should be confident working across methods An understanding of how organizations communicate strategically, whether from working in communications, consulting, government, public affairs, journalism or a research-facing role Experience or genuine interest in how research connects to real-world decisions, whether that is corporate strategy, public policy, reputation management or something else entirely The ability to manage multiple workstreams simultaneously without dropping the ball MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS Bachelor’s degree Strong academic record At least 1.5 years of relevant professional experience Proficiency in Microsoft Word, PowerPoint and Excel. Familiarity with AI tools and a willingness to integrate them into day-to-day work REQUIRED APPLICATION MATERIALS Resume Cover letter (please speak to your experience with research and how you think about translating findings into clear, strategic recommendations for a senior audience. Please include one example of a project where you worked on a communications issue - blind or redact specifics as needed.) Writing sample (5-10 pages) Closing Date : 28 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates No specific closing time, we will start with interviews immediately. Application Details https://ats.rippling.com/quadrant-strategies/jobs/dcce551a-eb6d-4bbe-8288-a4c22012cb50 Apply via this link submitting their resume and cover letter. Website : https://quadrantstrategies.com/
item.dc:creator: Quadrant Strategies

item.title: Chief of Staff
item.description: Monica Harding MP is looking for an experienced, organised and politically astute Chief of Staff to lead her parliamentary and constituency office. The Chief of Staff will provide strategic advice, manage the office team, oversee parliamentary and constituency operations, and act as a trusted senior point of contact for stakeholders. The role requires excellent judgement, discretion, resilience and the ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-moving political environment. Key Responsibilities: • Lead the day-to-day management of Monica’s parliamentary and constituency office, ensuring systems, workloads and priorities are well organised. • Provide strategic advice to Monica on parliamentary, constituency and stakeholder priorities. • Coordinate Monica’s diary, briefings and forward planning in collaboration with relevant team members. • Build and maintain strong relationships with local stakeholders, community groups, parliamentary colleagues, councils, public bodies and other partners. • Support Monica’s work in Parliament, including preparation for debates, questions, meetings, media opportunities and policy work. • Manage and support staff, including overseeing performance, development, wellbeing and recruitment where required. • Horizon-scan political, local and parliamentary developments, identifying opportunities and risks. Person Specification We are looking for someone who can demonstrate: • Significant experience in Parliament, politics, public affairs, policy, communications, campaigns, local government, the charity sector or a similarly fast-paced environment. • Excellent political judgement and a strong understanding of Parliament, government and public policy. • Experience managing people, projects or complex workloads. • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills. • Strong organisational skills, with the ability to prioritise under pressure and meet deadlines. • Sound judgement, discretion and the ability to handle sensitive or confidential information. • A calm, practical and solutions-focused approach. • A commitment to high standards of constituency service. • Sympathy with Liberal Democrat values and Monica’s priorities for Esher and Walton as well as International Development. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 15 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates We may appoint before the closing dates. We are open to consider applications for a part time role. Application Details Please use the subject line: Chief of Staff Application – [Your Name] Candidates may be asked to complete a short written or practical exercise as part of the process. Due to the volume of applications, we may only be able to contact shortlisted candidates. 1. A CV of no more than two pages. 2. A covering letter of no more than two pages setting out your suitability for the role. to: alice.dabrowski@parliament.uk Website : http://www.monicaharding.org/
item.dc:creator: Monica Harding MP (Esher and Walton)

item.title: Policy Manager
item.description: Role purpose To manage the programme’s policy activity and provide support and expertise to help ensure effective delivery of the programme’s strategy to influence policy change. Working closely with internal and external stakeholders, the role will plan and deliver activities to help ensure Trussell’s analysis and arguments are taken up and the organisation’s policy recommendations are reflected in decisions made by UK government, in line with the programme goals. This role is part of Trussell’s Making Social Security Work programme, the goal of which is to ensure that social security and employment support are accessible, well-designed and sufficiently funded in order to prevent people experiencing hunger and hardship, and that the UK public view social security as an asset to society. This role is focused on the successful delivery of the overall programme outcomes, contributing to the fulfilment of our long term vision of a UK without the need for food banks. Closing Date : 27 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Closing time - 9am Interview Date - 12 August 2026 Application Details To apply, please visit: https://www.trussell.org.uk/jobs/policy-manager Website : http://trussell.org.uk
item.dc:creator: Trussell

item.title: Caseworker
item.description: Job Details • Engage professionally, confidentially with care and integrity with members of the public, through telephone enquiries, emails, face to face and social media. • Ensure all cases are logged; monitor progress and ensure all identified actions are taken. • Ensure records are kept and information managed confidentially and in line with data protection legislation. • Exercise sound judgement in reaching decisions on cases based on the evidence. • Assist with surgeries and other meetings and follow up action as appropriate. • Gather relevant information to resolve or progress cases. • Liaise with Government agencies, the voluntary sector and others to resolve constituency matters. • Manage a large caseload of complex cases, working in a professional, confidential, caring and sensitive manner. • Provide briefings for the MP. • Respond professionally, sensitively, confidentially and with integrity to routine correspondence and enquiries by email, telephone, social media and face to face from constituents, government departments, public and private sector organisations, the media and campaign organisations. • There may be a requirement for the role holder to work outside of normal hours and travel for work occasionally. Key Skills Previous experience of carrying out casework working in an MP’s office or another organisation (desirable) Excellent written and oral communication skills (required) Attention to detail (required) The ability to work often under pressure and to tight deadlines (required) Excellent time-management and team working skills (required) Excellent organisational skills Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 14 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will be Thursday 16th July 2026 PM. Application Details Please email your CV (maximum length two pages) and a one-page cover letter outlining why you want the role and why you would be a suitable candidate. Please email your application to Carl Cuss, Head of Office, email: carlj.cuss@parliament.uk Website : https://nick-smith.net/
item.dc:creator: Nick Smith MP (Blaenau Gwent & Rhymney)
