item.title: Research Fellow (Place Effects) (Fixed Term)
item.description: Fixed-term: The funds for this post are available for 24 months. Applications are invited for a full-time Research Associate to join BRIDGE, a five-year, €2 million European Research Council (ERC) Consolidator Grant led by Dr Davide Luca, investigating urban-rural political polarisation. The successful candidate will join an international and interdisciplinary research team comprising the Principal Investigator, Project Manager, Research Fellows and Research Assistant, and will contribute to pioneering research examining the causes and consequences of urban-rural divides. The team also hosts international academic visitors throughout the duration of the project. The post holder will contribute across the project, with a particular focus on Work Package 2, which examines the drivers of outlook polarisation along the urban-rural continuum and seeks to disentangle compositional from place effects. This work will involve analysing large-scale longitudinal household panel surveys, applying advanced quantitative and causal inference methods, and developing innovative research designs to investigate how early-life exposure to key geographical characteristics influences later-life attitudes and behaviours. The position is full-time with flexible working arrangements and is available on a fixed-term basis for 24 months. The role is primarily intended for post-doctoral researchers, although exceptionally strong pre-doctoral candidates whose doctoral research closely aligns with the BRIDGE project may also be considered. To apply online for this vacancy and to view further information about the role, please click on the ‘Apply’ button above. The closing date for applications is: Midnight on Monday 3rd August. Interviews are expected to take place between mid-August and mid-September. Informal enquiries are welcomed and should be directed to: Dr Davide Luca Email: dl622@cam.ac.uk If you have any queries regarding the application process, please contact: hr@hsps.cam.ac.uk Please quote reference JP50176 on your application and in any correspondence about this vacancy. The University actively supports equality, diversity and inclusion and encourages applications from all sections of society. The University has a responsibility to ensure that all employees are eligible to live and work in the UK.
item.dc:creator: University of Cambridge

item.title: Political Assistant - Labour Group
item.description: Work location: George Meehan House, 294 High Road, Wood Green, N22 8XY Hours per week: 36 per week Contract type: Fixed term contract (Till September 2027) An opportunity has arisen to support the Council’s Principal Opposition Group as it holds the Council’s executive to account. The Group Assistant is a unique role in the Council, in that it directly supports the Labour group by undertaking research and suggesting questions, drafting media releases and supporting the Group’s meetings with agenda planning and minutes taking. Also supporting Labour Councillors’ participation in meetings of the Council and Committees. You will be a strong self-starter, able to work alone, with a good understanding of local government and current issues in Haringey. You should be able to give sound, clear advice on complex issues with a high degree of political sensitivity. You should be able to act at pace and work effectively with elected members, senior officers within the Council and external stakeholders. You will also need to have good IT skills and have proficiency in Microsoft Office applications as well as a good understanding of online meeting applications. You should be a member of the Labour party and must not be a member of any other party. This post is mainly remote working and will entail attendance at evening meetings, for which time off is given or overtime payable. Assistants must work within the procedures contained in the Council’s Constitution and Officer Code of Conduct. The posts are subject to the limitations set by the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 and Local authority Guidance Political assistance Guidance dated 11th October 2021. item.title: Policy & Senior Policy Manager - Energy, Climate & Environment
item.description: Policy Manager: We are recruiting a Policy Manager to work across all our energy and climate policy projects. This is an ideal role for someone with strong intellectual curiosity and policy instincts, who wants to develop original ideas and turn them into compelling analysis and arguments that influence decision-makers. You will have spent two or more years in a political, policy, research, consultancy or a closely-related field; you will have some experience taking charge of a project and seeing it through to completion; and be confident emailing and calling clients and sector experts. Senior Policy Manager: We are recruiting a Senior Policy Manager to play a leading role in delivering and shaping our Energy, Climate and Environment policy work. This role is suited to someone with substantial experience — typically three to five years — in policy, research, consultancy or a closely-related field, who is ready to take ownership of projects and act as a trusted adviser to clients. Senior Policy Managers at Public First lead projects day-to-day, manage and support more junior colleagues, and make a strong intellectual contribution to the firm's work. You will work closely with Associate Directors and Directors to design projects, develop insights and arguments, and support the growth and profile of our Energy, Climate and Environment practice. For full descriptions of our roles, please follow the provided links. Closing Date : 17 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Stage 1 will be a 30-minute online interview. Stage 2 will be an hour-long in-person interview at 20 Victoria Street SW1H 0NB. item.title: Associate Account Director - Defence
item.description: CMS Strategic is a London-based specialist communications agency for the global defence, aerospace and security sectors. At a key time of growth, an opportunity is now available for an Associate Account Director to join the team.The successful candidate must be highly motivated, personable, professional, discrete and able to manage a variety of responsibilities to support our busy and productive agency. The Associate Account Director will lead on a selection of client accounts, develop strategic communications plans, and ensure the successful delivery of high-quality public relations and public affairs campaigns. This role is responsible for managing client relationships, overseeing account teams, driving business growth, and delivering measurable results that align with clients' commercial and reputational objectives. Key Responsibilities Client leadership Strategic Communications Public Affairs Media Relations Team Leadership Account Management Business Development Skills and Experience Essential • 4+ years senior experience in public relations, communications, or corporate affairs, preferably in Defence. • A strong knowledge of UK politics, Parliament, Whitehall, government departments, and the public affairs landscape. • Demonstrable experience of delivering integrated communications campaigns, public affairs strategy, parliamentary engagement and reputation management. • Experience managing multiple high-profile client accounts. • Excellent strategic planning and problem-solving skills. • Strong media relations and political network. • Outstanding written, verbal, and presentation skills. • Proven leadership and people management experience. Key Competencies • Strategic thinking • Client relationship management • Commercial awareness • Leadership and coaching • Influencing and negotiation • Excellent communication skills • Project management • Creativity and innovation Benefits • Central London location with flexibility to work from home 1 day per week and ad hoc as needed • Competitive salary with performance-related bonus • Reservist policy • Training and professional development • Expense account • Health insurance • A sociable team with regular group outings and social events • Showering facilities and off-street bike storage Closing Date : 31 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews to commence immediately. Application Details To apply, please send your CV and a cover letter to: Oliver.Tillard@cmsstrategic.com Please submit CV and cover letter by 1700hrs Please describe clearly why you would like to join CMS Strategic and how your personal skillset meets our requirement. Examples of how you have delivered this type of work (respecting necessary sensitivities) is preferable. Please prepare to provide references. Website : http://www.cmsstrategic.com

item.title: Policy Adviser
item.description: We are looking for a Policy Adviser for maternity cover in the Policy Impact Unit, Department of STEaPP at UCL. The Policy Impact Unit (PIU) collaborates with researchers in the Faculty of Engineering Sciences (FES) at UCL to design bespoke policy engagement strategies that have real impact. Using innovative and adaptive approaches, the PIU makes policy engagement accessible for researchers and increases the use of UCL research in the policymaking process. The Policy Adviser will work closely with UCL academics, teasing out the ‘so what’ from their research projects and identifying policy implications. They will engage with policymakers in a proactive way on a range of related topics including artificial intelligence and robotics. The successful applicant will be an excellent communicator, both verbally and in writing. They will have policy experience and a good understanding of the policy landscape, able to identify opportunities for policy engagement and act on them. They should be able to ‘speak the language’ of policymakers and be able to understand complex information. They should have experience designing engagement events, and a keen interest in working at the interface of academic and policy – as well as in technology policy. If you have any queries regarding the vacancy or the application process, please contact Kate Wallis on kate.wallis@ucl.ac.uk Closing Date : 13 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Reference number B04-07514 Full time 36.5 hours per week - hybrid working. Fixed term: 1 year. Start date is August 2026, end date August 2027. Application Details To apply please visit https://www.ucl.ac.uk/work-at-ucl/search-ucl-jobs/details?jobId=45779&jobTitle=Policy+Adviser Your UCL job application • When applying for a job we will often request you include a ‘supporting statement’ along with a CV or application form. Website : https://www.ucl.ac.uk/

item.title: Communications Officer
item.description: Kate Osborne MP is looking to recruit a creative, proactive and highly organised Communications Officer to join her constituency team. This is an exciting opportunity to support the work of an active local MP, communicating Kate’s parliamentary, and constituency work clearly and effectively to residents, community organisations, media and other stakeholders. The postholder will produce engaging digital, press and written content across a range of platforms. They will help develop and deliver a proactive communications strategy that reflects local priorities, promotes community and ensures that constituents are kept informed about Kate’s work. The role will be based in the constituency and will involve accompanying Kate on visits, meetings, community events and other engagements across Jarrow and Gateshead East. Key responsibilities: Establish, monitor and update a social media and online presence in the constituency. Produce online graphics and video content, as well as publicising surgeries and news alerts etc. Follow up on social media queries and comments Manage and monitor social media enquiries, dealing with cases that do not need to be transferred to a higher level. Ensure all enquiries are dealt with in a timely manner Monitor media coverage, liaise with media, prepare press releases as required (on constituency, non-party-political matters) Produce press releases, liaise with the media, meeting deadlines as appropriate Analyse patterns of enquiries and produce reports Attend constituency meetings as appropriate Critically analyse patterns of enquiries and articulate the findings in reports and communication with the MP and other staff members Retain records and information confidentially and in line with the Data Protection Act There may be a requirement for the role holder to work outside of the normal working hours and travel for work occasionally Additional responsibilities: Organise and attend constituency outreach events and surgeries. Provide briefings, research and speechwriting support where required. Carry out other reasonable duties in support of the MP and the wider office. Full person specification can be found on Kate's website: https://kate-osborne.co.uk/vacancies/ Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 31 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will take place the week commencing 10th August 2026 Expected start date : September Application Details Full job advertisement can be found on Kate's website: https://kate-osborne.co.uk/vacancies/ Please submit a CV and covering letter explaining your interest in the position and the skills and experience you would bring to the role. Applicants are also encouraged to provide 2-5 examples of relevant written, digital, photographic or video content they have produced. This can be as simple as a Facebook post, or a press release or video content. Applications should be sent to: kate.osborne.mp@parliament.uk Website : https://kate-osborne.co.uk/

item.title: Caseworker
item.description: Blake Stephenson MP is seeking a full-time, hardworking, skilled and motivated Caseworker, to be based in his Mid-Beds constituency office. Hybrid arrangements would be considered for the right candidate with occasional travel to the constituency. The successful candidate will be proactive, with strong writing, communication, AI, IT and organisational skills. The role also requires a flexible attitude and the ability to manage constituency casework, campaign emails and correspondence. Previous experience in a similar role is strongly preferred. Key responsibilities Casework oversight Managing a casework caseload, including complex and priority cases Managing a campaign caseload Drafting letters, emails and correspondence to constituents Continuous learning and development reviews to improve systems for triaging, recording and progressing casework Helping to develop templates, standard responses and internal guidance where appropriate Contributing to regular casework reviews and team discussions Analysis and campaign links Identifying patterns, themes and recurring issues arising from constituency casework to help inform the MP’s local campaigns, policy work and stakeholder engagement Producing briefings for the MP and office team on emerging casework trends Skills and qualities Strong writing, communication and organisational skills, and able to take the initiative Hardworking, flexible, proactive team player, with the ability to work to tight deadlines Excellent IT skills and an understanding of responsible AI use Strong attention to detail and an ability to prioritise workload Experience managing complex public-facing enquiries and processes Ability to work independently and contribute constructively as part of a small team Ability to use casework management software Politically aware, with an interest in current affairs Sympathetic to the aims and values of the Conservative Party Benefits Generous employer pension contribution Access to training and development opportunities Employee Assistance Programme Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 27 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will take place on a rolling basis and an appointment may be made prior to the closing date; we would therefore encourage early applications Application Details Apply online at https://recruitment.hivesupport.co.uk/job/35384/caseworker-to-blake-stephenson-mp Website : https://www.blakestephenson.uk/
item.dc:creator: Blake Stephenson MP (Mid Bedfordshire)

item.title: Content and Communications Coordinator
item.description: We seek to appoint a creative, innovative, and highly motivated individual to coordinate and implement our content and communications activities across our numerous platforms. The candidate will have experience in creativity, communications, social media, and website management. The successful applicant will have a graduate or postgraduate degree and a keen interest in strengthening the national power and profile of the United Kingdom, as well as foreign affairs and international security more generally. The Content and Communications Coordinator will be an integral part of our organisation’s team. The successful applicant will involve themselves in creating graphic and video products, developing and managing social media campaigns for our organisation and projects, running our social media accounts, and maintaining our website. This role is an excellent opportunity for the successful applicant to immerse themselves in the world of Westminster and promote the strengthening of Britain domestically and internationally. Please see the tasks associated with the role and requirements on our website: https://www.geostrategy.org.uk/content-and-communications-coordinator/ Closing Date : 2 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates Successful candidates will be invited for an online interview, followed by a task and in-person interview. Interviews will be scheduled on a rolling basis, so early applications are encouraged. Expected starting date: August 2026 Application Details Integrated two-page application letter and CV. The first page must include the application letter, while the second page must contain the CV. Both should be drafted either in Arial or Georgia (Size: 12 point) and submitted as a single PDF file. The letter should explain why you would like the role, while the CV should contain the following sub-headings: 1. Academic achievements; 2. Work experience; 3. Any other relevant experience; 4. Referees (listing at least two) sent to careers@geostrategy.org.uk Website : https://www.geostrategy.org.uk/
item.dc:creator: Council on Geostrategy

item.title: Caseworker
item.description: Sarah Dyke MP is seeking a Part-Time Caseworker to join her busy constituency office team, working three days per week. This is an exciting opportunity to support the Liberal Democrat spokesperson for Rural Affairs, helping constituents across a large rural constituency while contributing to effective representation on the issues that matter most to local communities. The successful candidate will be able to work independently in a fast-paced environment, maintaining high standards of accuracy, professionalism and confidentiality. Key Responsibilities Assist at constituency surgeries and other meetings and undertake follow-up work as appropriate. Deal with large numbers of standard queries, as well as assisting with more complex cases from members of the public by phone, email or face-to-face. Draft responses to constituents and stakeholders. Ensure records are kept and information is managed confidentially in line with data protection legislation. Ensure that each case is dealt with promptly, sensitively, confidentially and accurately in accordance with the MP's policies and procedures. Gather relevant information to assist with resolving cases. Log all cases, monitor progress and ensure all identified actions are taken. Liaise with government departments, local authorities and other organisations on behalf of constituents where appropriate. Retain records and information confidentially and in line with the Data Protection Act. There may be a requirement for the role holder to work outside normal working hours and travel occasionally for work. Essential Criteria Ability to deal with and prioritise a large and varied workload and work well under pressure. Excellent interpersonal and communication skills. Good IT skills, including Microsoft Office. Ability to draft letters and emails to a high professional standard. Motivation to work proactively and independently, as well as part of a small team. Commitment to the aims and values of the Liberal Democrats. Desirable Criteria Experience in casework, advocacy or a related field. An interest in rural affairs and an understanding of the opportunities and challenges facing rural communities. Knowledge of local government, public services or community organisations. For an informal discussion about the role, please contact Helen White at helen.white@parliament.uk Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 17 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates First Tier Interview - 23rd July 2026 Second Tier Interview - 30th July 2026 Application Details Apply to sarah.dyke.MP@parliament.uk submitting CV and Covering Letter of no more than one page. Please note applications may close early. Website : http://www.sarahdyke.uk
item.dc:creator: Sarah Dyke MP (Glastonbury and Somerton)

item.title: Political Advisor
item.description: Your role and responsibilities This is an exciting and influential role for someone with a passion for politics, public service and communications. As Political Advisor, you will provide strategic advice, policy research and communications support to the Reform UK Group, supporting Members in analysing complex issues, developing policy positions, and making informed decisions as the Council’s majority political group. From preparing high-quality briefings and analysing emerging policy developments to supporting media engagement and social media activity, you will be at the centre of political decision-making within one of the largest local authorities in the country. Working closely with elected Members, senior officers and external stakeholders, you will help ensure the Group is well-informed, effectively coordinated and able to champion its priorities on behalf of Suffolk residents. No two days will be the same, offering a unique opportunity to influence local government policy, respond to emerging challenges and contribute to the future direction of Suffolk. You will need A strong understanding of politics, public policy and the role of local government. Excellent research, analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex information. Outstanding written and verbal communication skills, including experience producing briefings, reports or public-facing communications. Political awareness, sound judgement and the ability to operate confidently in a politically sensitive environment. Experience building productive relationships with senior stakeholders, elected Members or partners. Knowledge of media relations, social media and effective public communications. Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage competing priorities and tight deadlines. A proactive, resilient and self-motivated approach, with the confidence to work independently when required. Link to apply: https://eoce.fa.em3.oraclecloud.com/hcmUI/CandidateExperience/en/sites/CX_3001/job/24218/?utm_medium=jobshare&utm_source=External+Job+Share Closing Date : 20 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interview date: week commencing 27 July 2026 Start date: as soon as possible Job closes 11.30pm Application Details Please apply via Suffolk County Councils jobs website: https://eoce.fa.em3.oraclecloud.com/hcmUI/CandidateExperience/en/sites/CX_3001/job/24218/?utm_medium=jobshare&utm_source=External+Job+Share Website : http://www.suffolk.gov.uk/
item.dc:creator: Suffolk County Council - Reform UK Group

item.title: Events Manager
item.description: The Education Policy Institute (EPI) is seeking an experienced and motivated candidate to join our Communications, Partnerships and Events team as Events Manager. This position offers a fantastic opportunity to contribute to the success of EPI, directly supporting our mission to influence and improve policymaking, and ultimately to improve the life chances of children and young people in England. The role will lead on the delivery of our sector-leading convening programme, including overseeing production of policy papers based on our convening and wider quantitative research. You will: Have oversight of a key income stream, comprising: Managing delivery of our sector-leading events programme, bringing together decision makers, sector experts and parliamentarians to further policy debates and disseminate the work of EPI and our partners. Work alongside the Associate Director for Partnerships and Events in maintaining and growing a portfolio of high-profile EPI partners. Oversee production of high-quality policy papers alongside the convening, drawing together sector views with EPI research to influence education policy Report to our Associate Director for Partnerships and Events, whilst also working closely with the CEO and wider Research teams About EPI The Education Policy Institute is one of the country’s leading public policy research organisations. We aim to promote high quality education outcomes for all children and young people, regardless of social background. About you Your work at the Education Policy Institute will build on the experience you already have. You will: Have at least three years’ experience working in an events role, including experience overseeing the whole event lifecycle Have exceptional written communication skills, with experience of writing policy briefs desirable Be confident leading meetings with partners, and speaking authoritatively about your work Have at least a year’s management experience Be able to work at pace and comfortable working to competing priorities Have a good knowledge of education policy Be committed to EPI’s mission to improve outcomes for disadvantaged children and young people through timely, impactful and evidence-based analysis For further information, please see the full job advert here: https://epi.org.uk/jobs/events-manager/ Closing Date : 28 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will take place in our London office during week commencing 10th August, though we can be flexible with timing and the option of a virtual interview for the right candidate. Application Details To apply, please send us your CV with a covering letter, of no more than two sides, addressing the competencies above, to info@epi.org.uk by 11.59pm on the closing date The interview will cover your skills and experience. The interview panel will include the Associate Director for Partnerships and Events, a representative from the Research team, as well as an additional panel member from outside the organisation. If you have any questions about the post, the application process or require any reasonable adjustments, please do contact info@epi.org.uk before applying. Website : https://epi.org.uk/
item.dc:creator: Education Policy Institute

item.title: Field Organiser - South East
item.description: Salary: £32,246.69 (gross) per annum, with a fixed sum allowance of £1,195. There is the ability to progress to £36,211.53 (gross) per annum over the next 3 years. This is a Regular Driver Role so also attracts a car allowance of £5000 (gross) per annum, subject to internal checks. Current employees in banding 15-21 will maintain their existing salary. The Labour Party is looking to recruit a Field Organiser – South East. The post-holders will be responsible for all aspects of Labour Party organisation and campaigning within a number of electorally important areas, as determined by the Regional Manager. The post holders will be responsible for building and maintaining party organisation and activity; establishing effective election and campaign organisation working in liaison with staff teams, local candidates, elected representatives, Labour Party officers, campaign committees, Constituency Labour Parties, Branches, Labour Groups and other units within the Party. The successful candidate will have: Knowledge and understanding of the Party organisation at branch, constituency and regional level Experience of organising a campaign Experience of working with volunteers Ability to work on own initiative with minimum supervision and to deliver work to agreed deadlines Please note that this is a Regular Driver Role and there is a requirement to be able to drive and own a compliant car in this role. Any successful applicant will be required to drive and have a compliant car within 12 months of their start date. A driving licence is required for this role due to operational requirements including travel between multiple locations and transportation of campaign materials. Candidates who cannot drive due to disability are encouraged to apply as reasonable adjustments will be considered in line with the Equality Act 2010. The Labour Party values equality and diversity, and welcomes applications from candidates regardless of sex, race, disability, age, sexual orientation, gender reassignment, religion or belief, marriage and civil partnership, or pregnancy and maternity. Closing Date : 19 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interview dates to be confirmed. Application Details To find out more and apply to this vacancy, please see the original listing on our careers page: https://api.occupop.com/shared/job/field-organiser-south-east-267ab/W4MP Website : http://www.labour.org.uk
item.dc:creator: The Labour Party

item.title: Senior Administrative Officer
item.description: The role of Senior Administrative Officer will include the following responsibilities, based in the Westminster Office. Open, handle and action email, post, and social media enquiries. Respond to enquiries by telephone, letter, email, and social media, passing on queries to another team members/MP as appropriate. Diary management, minute taking and record keeping of meetings. Photocopying, filing, record-keeping and typing correspondence Assist in the compilation of subject briefing packs. Assist with arrangements for events – this may include venue research and bookings; compiling and managing guest-lists; liaising with suppliers; and providing on-the-day event support as required. Undertake desk research. Update MP's website and social media accounts with stories about Parliamentary and constituency activity and campaigns. Assist with the production/editing of videos for social media. *Video editing experience preferable. Monitor local, regional, and national media coverage and brief the MP on relevant issues. Manage production of parliamentary newsletters/mailouts. Have a working knowledge of relevant legislation and parliamentary business and understand how these policies may impact on local constituents. Draft press releases. Ensure the Westminster office is fully equipped with the required office supplies. Complete other administrative tasks required for the effective operation of the MP's Parliamentary office, eg record keeping of meetings. There may be a requirement for the role holder to work outside of the normal working hours and travel for work occasionally, eg: to the constituency. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 14 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews: Monday 20th July 2026. Start date: September 2026. Application Details Please send a CV and covering letter explaining your suitability for the role to mark.pritchard.mp@parliament.uk Applications received after the deadline will not be considered or receive a response. If you have any questions about the role, please email mark.pritchard.mp@parliament.uk rather than contacting the office by phone. Website : https://www.markpritchard.com/
item.dc:creator: The Rt Hon Mark Pritchard MP (The Wrekin)

item.description: We are seeking a Caritas Coordinator to support community engagement, communications, and events across the Archdiocese of Southwark. The role involves building partnerships with parishes and organisations, managing social media, and coordinating social action initiatives. Place of Work: Bowen House, 59 Westminster Bridge Road, London, SE1 7JE, with hybrid working and regular travel across the Archdiocese Hours: 35 hours per week, primarily over 5 days. Regular evening and weekend work will be required, with time off in lieu granted. item.title: Development Manager
item.description: Founded in 1977, the Adam Smith Institute (ASI) is one of the world's leading think tanks. Independent, non-profit and non-partisan, we work to promote free markets and economics, through research, publishing, media outreach, and public education. We inject sound ideas into public debate and help shift the climate of opinion. The ASI has always been a practical think tank rather than a purely academic organization. Despite our strict political independence, we work with policymakers to deliver real change, and to make its ideas reality. We are currently recruiting an experienced Development Manager to lead the ASI’s fundraising, external affairs and donor engagement efforts. To find the right fit for our team, we are fully open to both full-time and part-time arrangements. You will manage grant applications to foundations, as well as charitable donations from private individuals. You will also be responsible for developing a fundraising strategy, as well as cultivating relationships with key donors, together with the Chairman and senior staff. View the full job ad here: https://www.adamsmith.org/development-manager Closing Date : 31 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will begin during the week of 20 July 2026 so please do apply ASAP. We are looking to hire the right applicant and are flexible on the start date (but can offer an immediate start). Application Details View the full job ad here: https://www.adamsmith.org/development-manager This includes the form. Website : http://adamsmith.org
item.dc:creator: Adam Smith Institute

item.title: Researcher (Scotland) Maternity Cover
item.description: The Parliamentary Research Service (PRS) is a pooled research facility for Labour MPs. PRS is currently recruiting for this role: Researcher (Scotland) Key skills: • Excellent written communication • Strong analytical and research skills • Good oral communication and networking skills • Ability to interact comfortably with senior politicians • Numeracy and an understanding of data and statistics • Knowledge of Parliament, and UK and Scottish politics • The ability work to tight deadlines in a fast paced environment Key responsibilities: • Drafting policy-related correspondence replies to constituents • Drafting briefing notes on issues • Provide support for other parliamentary activity • Willingness to accept critical feedback and to learn Requirements: • Existing documented right to work in the UK • Experience in policy, parliamentary or a relevant business environment is desirable, but we are also looking for potential and aptitude • Fluent English You should bear in mind that PRS’s subscribers are Labour MPs and you must be comfortable supporting them in their parliamentary duties. The first four months is a probationary period in which you will undertake an assessment process for which we provide the necessary training and support throughout. After the probationary period you will continue to complete the contract subject to reaching required performance levels. Closing Date : 14 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interview/Start Dates Interviews: 18-22 July Start date: 24/08/26 Application Details CV and covering letter addressed to PRS Director Dan Whittle to prs@parliament.uk Website : https://parliamentaryresearch.com

item.description: London & Partners is seeking a Senior Policy Manager to play a leading role in shaping policy and thought leadership focused on London’s economic growth. The role offers the opportunity to work on high-profile projects spanning areas such as innovation, climate, life sciences, AI and business growth, while engaging closely with senior stakeholders across government, industry and the wider London ecosystem. https://www.ellwoodatfield.com/jobs/senior-policy-manager-h3bpp Closing Date : 16 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Application Details https://www.ellwoodatfield.com/jobs/senior-policy-manager-h3bpp Website : https://www.ellwoodatfield.com/about-us

item.description: Emma Foody MP is looking for a highly motivated individual with excellent organisational and communication skills to join her constituency office as a caseworker. The role will involve supporting Emma’s work in the Cramlington and Killingworth constituency and responding to and supporting constituents. The role is part time on a six month fixed term contract. Job Description Deal with large numbers of standard queries, as well as assisting on more complex cases from members of the public by phone, email, social media or face to face. Draft responses to constituents Assist surgeries and other meetings and follow up as appropriate Attend constituency meetings as appropriate Critically analyse patterns of enquiries and articulate the findings in reports and communication with the MP and other staff members Ensure records are kept and information managed confidentially in line with the data protection legislation Ensure that each case is dealt with promptly, sensitively, confidentially and accurately in accordance with the MP Gather relevant information to assist with resolving cases Log all cases; monitor progress and ensure all identified actions are taken Retain records and information confidentially and in line with the Data Protection Act There may be a requirement for the role holder to work outside of the normal working hours and travel for work occasionally Key Skills and Experience Previous knowledge and experience of casework desirable Organised, attention to detail, conscientious, empathetic Strong multi-tasking and time management skills Capacity to handle a high and complex workload Strong written and verbal communication skills Ability to work independently and as part of a small team Sympathy with the aims and values of the Labour Party Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). item.title: Senior Research Officer
item.description: Propertymark’s research operation exists to power the housing sector with wide ranging insight that gives clear evidence and statistical awareness on key subject matter. Housing is one of the very few subjects that has a universal appeal for almost every person across the entire UK. Our forward-thinking ambition regarding data collection and analysis stands directly in the middle of our desire to create an innovative and stronger housing sector that delivers for the future. You will work with colleagues across the business to design, implement, process and report on research concepts that bring value and knowledge for those who work within the sector, and specifically our membership base. Your ideas and experience will be target driven, and you will possess a passion for engaging with stakeholders to deliver information and reporting that creates impact. https://www.propertymark.co.uk/about-us/jobs-at-propertymark.html Closing Date : 13 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Application Details Please send your CV and covering letter to jobs@propertymark.co.uk Website : https://www.propertymark.co.uk

item.title: Client Executive/Manager
item.description: Tripoint is a strategic communications consultancy built for the real assets investment industry, working with asset owners, fund managers, and advisers across real estate, natural capital, and infrastructure - all asset classes highly sensitive to politics, policy, and regulation. This is an opportunity to join a challenger business at ground level, shaping how we work and supporting market-leading clients active in the UK, Europe, North America, and Asia-Pacific. We are hiring across two levels as we build our team: 1) Client Executive: Entry-level (around 1 year of communications or PR experience). Supports client accounts across media relations, content creation, and reporting. 2) Client Manager: Emerging career role (at least 2 years of experience). Takes day-to-day ownership of client accounts, leads media outreach, and helps shape client strategy. What You'll Be Doing You will gain exposure to the full range of Tripoint’s services, including: Corporate narrative, messaging, and industry positioning Corporate communications and media relations Content marketing, thought leadership, and research Digital communications, campaigning, and investor communications Crisis/issues management and platform launches What We're Looking For Genuine interest in real assets, investment, or financial/professional services Strong written/verbal communication skills and digital channel literacy (e.g., LinkedIn) A proactive, entrepreneurial mindset suited to a start-up environment Graphic design skills are a plus What We Offer Direct exposure to clients and a real seat at the table Office-based (Chancery Lane) with 1 day/week remote flexibility Gym membership and 28 days' annual leave (excluding bank holidays), scaling over time Closing Date : 28 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Rolling interviews from when we receive applications. We may appoint someone before the closing date. Application Details Send your CV to partners@tripointstrategic.com , letting us know which role you're applying f or. We will only respond to applicants we are interested in interviewing. Website : http://www.tripointstrategic.com

item.title: Account Executive
item.description: We're looking for a smart, enthusiastic, and organised graduate looking to kickstart their career in PR and politics. About Quatro Quatro is a market leader in providing political and community engagement for the property, energy and infrastructure sectors. We have a young, dynamic team who do fantastic work across a range of projects including: · urban renewal · new settlement proposals · large-scale renewable energy schemes · strategic engagement with metro mayors and the devolution agenda. We are based in Holborn, central London, in a modern, vibrant office space that makes for a great working environment, offering plenty of social activities including lunch time yoga, weekly beer and pizza, local discounts, and event evenings. The role We are on the hunt for an Account Executive to join our growing, dynamic team. The successful candidate will work across projects in the property and energy sectors. Working in small teams, you will be responsible for research, media monitoring, and project support. You’ll get experience of a range of different activities, developing a great PR and politics skillset. The role will offer the right candidate the chance to gain considerable experience and skills over an 18-24 month period, with opportunities for career development and progression. Your skills Writing – You’ll be able to draft high-quality copy for engagement materials and concise briefing notes. Research – You’ll have strong research skills, able to gather and summarise information from diverse sources. Digital – You’ll have an interest in social media as a tool for communications and campaigns. Politics – You’ll be passionate about politics and current affairs. You could have experience as a volunteer in campaigning. Organisation – You’ll be able to manage your time effectively, dealing with multiple projects and competing deadlines. What you’ll get A competitive salary, pension contributions, and 25 days leave. There will be opportunities for hybrid working once embedded in the team. We’ll help the right candidate develop their career, with opportunities for training and growth. Closing Date : 17 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates In terview/Start Dates Early applications are strongly encouraged as interviews will take place on a rolling basis and we may appoint before the closing date. Application Details Application Details Please send your CV and a short covering letter to Richard Parry at rparry@quatro-pr.co.uk We would particularly welcome applications from underrepresented groups, including women, people who consider themselves to have a disability and people from a BAME background. We expect to receive a large number of applications, so it may not be possible to contact all applicants who are not shortlisted for interview. Website : http://www.quatro-pr.co.uk

item.title: Public Affairs, Policy and Membership Manager
item.description: Together for Short Lives supports families directly through practical guidance, emotional support and financial assistance, while also bringing together children's hospices, NHS services and health and care professionals to improve standards of care across the UK. The role: The Public Affairs, Policy and Membership Manager will play a key role in shaping their influencing strategy, supporting evidence-led policy work, and lead membership engagement to ensure their community feels valued and informed. Reporting to the Associate Director of External Affairs & Membership, you will monitor political and policy developments, develop briefings and consultation responses, and build relationships with members, parliamentarians, government officials and sector partners to help improve access to high-quality children's palliative care across the UK. Responsibilities: ‍ Lead Together for Short Lives' public affairs, policy and influencing activity, helping shape national policy to improve children's palliative care. Monitor political and policy developments, producing briefings, consultation responses and campaign materials for members and stakeholders. Build and maintain relationships with MPs, government officials, NHS bodies and sector partners, while supporting the APPG for Children Who Need Palliative Care. Represent the charity externally, translating research and evidence into impactful policy recommendations and influencing opportunities. Support the strategic development of the membership programme and line manage the Membership and Engagement Officer. Manage the organisation's finances, external suppliers, and day-to-day operations, ensuring long-term sustainability and growth. Key skills and experience required: ‍ Proven public affairs or policy experience, with the ability to influence government and shape policy. Strong understanding of the UK political landscape, with a passion for current affairs and an interest in healthcare, charities or membership organisations. Strong stakeholder engagement skills, building relationships with MPs, government officials, NHS bodies and sector partners. Excellent written and analytical skills, with experience producing policy briefings, consultation responses and evidence-based recommendations. Previous line management or experience or the confidence to step into a management role. Closing Date : 16 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interview: 22nd July Application Details To apply, please follow the link to our website and submit the application form: https://www.ellwoodatfield.com/jobs/public-affairs-policy-and-membership-manager Website : https://www.togetherforshortlives.org.uk/
item.dc:creator: Together for Short Lives

item.title: Parliamentary and Public Affairs Manager
item.description: BPAS is an independent healthcare charity which, for more than 55 years, has been advocating for and caring for women and couples who decide to end a pregnancy. We are the leading specialist provider of abortion advice and treatment in the UK, supporting over 100,000 women each year across more than 50 clinics nationwide. BPAS is committed to protecting, defending, and advancing abortion rights across the UK. Together our advocacy and campaigns team have delivered generational change in abortion law and access – including the decriminalisation of women, the introduction of safe access zones around clinics, and changing the law to allow telemedical abortion care. We are looking for a Parliamentary and Public Affairs Manager to join our small, effective, and highly committed advocacy team to help us take this essential work forward. This role forms part of a renewed investment in our advocacy function, providing an exciting opportunity to help shape BPAS's public affairs work at a significant time for reproductive health and rights. As Parliamentary and Public Affairs Manager, you will work closely with the Head of Advocacy and wider advocacy team to manage BPAS's relationships with parliamentarians, stakeholders and the wider pro-choice sector. You will play a key role in influencing policy and decision-makers across Parliament, Government and the wider political landscape to improve women’s rights, health, and access to abortion care. Link to the full advert: https://www.linkedin.com/talent/hire/1758466428/job-post?jobId=4436808842 Closing Date : 21 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Week commencing 3rd August 2026 at our Office in London Application Details Full details on how to apply can be found on the advert: https://www.linkedin.com/talent/hire/1758466428/job-post?jobId=4436808842 Website : http://www.bpas.org/
item.dc:creator: British Pregnancy Advisory Service

item.title: Research and Policy Officer
item.description: The Trust is recruiting a creative and committed Research and Policy Officer to join our team. Reporting to one of the team’s Research and Policy Managers, the successful candidate will play a significant role in developing and preparing the Trust’s research and policy outputs, working across the core areas of the Sutton Trust’s work (early years, schools, apprenticeships, higher education and access to the workplace). This will include helping to produce original, data rich and policy relevant research reports, along with contributing to other Trust research projects. They will also support the policy and advocacy work of the Trust, including performing background research on topics relevant to educational inequality and social mobility as required. Full information is available on our website: https://recruitment.suttontrust.com/jobs/8017266-research-and-policy-officer Closing Date : 24 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Applications should reach us by 5pm on the deadline date, with first round interviews held over Zoom on Monday 10th August, and second round interviews held at our London offices on Monday 17th August. Application Details To apply, please visit the link on our recruitment site: https://recruitment.suttontrust.com/jobs/8017266-research-and-policy-officer Website : https://www.suttontrust.com/

item.title: Wales Policy Officer
item.description: The National Education Union is a powerful force for change in education, representing 500,000 teachers, lecturers, support staff, and leaders. As we have recently completed an extensive staffing review, this is an exciting time to join us on our journey to becoming a more agile, strategic, and powerfully coordinated trade union – ready to meet the challenges of the future and drive lasting change in education. We are currently seeking to appoint a Wales Policy Officer on a part-time basis (24.5 hours per week) to assist in the promotion of the Union’s policies in the Wales Government, the Wales media and through supporting campaigning across Wales. In this role you’d need to have good knowledge and understanding of the education systems operating in Wales and in the UK and relevant legislation and be able to link it with the priorities, issues and concerns affecting NEU members in Wales. You will also need to be able to develop and maintain positive working relationships with internal and external stakeholders and be able to write clearly and concisely and present arguments with clarity. In addition, to be successful in this role, you’d need to have a good understanding of the principles of organising in a trade union and knowledge and understanding of campaigning activity. Due to the nature of this role, there will be a requirement to travel to attend activities, conferences and meetings away from the normal place of work, and which may involve stays away from home. These could be locally within Wales or nationally. Prospective applicants are also advised that your normal place of work will be the NEU office specified in this advert and that the NEU operates ‘Blended Working Arrangements’ for office-based staff, which means you will typically need to be based in the designated NEU office for 3 to 4 days a week (based on a full-time contract), with the remaining days based at home, and subject to business needs. In addition to salary, we offer good conditions including 33 days’ annual leave plus Christmas closure days and a defined benefit pension scheme. Salary and benefits will be calculated pro-rata for part-time applicants. THE UNION IS FUNDAMENTALLY COMMITTED TO EQUAL OPPORTUNIES IN ITS POLICIES AND PRACTICE Closing Date : 23 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Closing time is mid-day Application Details https://neu.org.uk/working-neu Website : https://neu.org.uk
item.dc:creator: National Education Union

item.title: PR intern
item.description: Are you looking for an opportunity to launch your career in PR? We are looking for 2 interns to join our team. Our three-month paid internship is designed to give aspiring communications professionals hands-on experience in strategic communications, corporate affairs, public relations, public affairs and reputation management. Whether you're a student, graduate, career changer or simply exploring a future in communications, this programme is intended for individuals seeking to start a career in communications. It is aimed at those with little or no professional experience in the sector and focuses on developing foundational skills. The programme combines hands-on experience with structured training to help you build practical communications skills. You’ll learn through a mix of live client work, team collaboration, feedback, mentoring and dedicated training sessions. You’ll have the opportunity to develop practical skills including: • Supporting client account teams across a range of sectors and specialisms • Monitoring media coverage, political developments and current affairs • Conducting research and stakeholder mapping • Preparing briefing materials, presentations and reports • Drafting content and communications materials • Supporting social media and digital communications activity • Supporting public affairs and stakeholder engagement activity • Assisting with new business and marketing projects • Attending internal meetings, training sessions and team events • Learning how strategic communications programmes are developed and delivered We're looking for people who demonstrate: • Curiosity about current affairs, media, politics and business • Strong communication skills • Good judgement • Adaptability and willingness to learn • Initiative and enthusiasm • An interest in communications and reputation You do not need previous communications or agency experience to apply. We encourage applications from individuals who may not previously have considered a career in communications and are committed to making our recruitment process inclusive and accessible. If you require reasonable adjustments at any stage of the application process, please let us know. Closing Date : 22 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews: 20 July to 31 July 2026 Assessment session: 11 August 2026 Start date: 7 September 2026 Application Details The application link below provides full details of the application process, including the qualifying questions to include in your application and instructions on where to submit it. https://lansons.com/2026-internship-programme Due to the large number of applications we receive, we are unable to response to every applicant. Website : https://lansons.com/
item.dc:creator: Lansons Team Farner

item.title: Housing Engagement and Inclusion Officer
item.description: This is an exciting opportunity to join Runnymede Borough Council as we continue our positive work in the Housing Service. Runnymede Borough Council is a social landlord to 3,000 households. Our Housing Engagement and Inclusion Officer plays a key role in ensuring tenants have a strong voice in shaping our services. They make sure tenant feedback is actively considered and that we clearly communicate how this has influenced housing services. This is an exciting time to join Runnymede as we build on the strong progress already made in developing tenant-led engagement. The Engagement and Inclusion Officer is responsible for: Increasing the level, channels and quality of meaningful opportunities to influence and scrutinise the Council’s Housing strategies, policies and services. Assisting tenants who wish to implement tenant-led activities to influence and scrutinise our strategies, policies and services. Identifying opportunities, and providing support, so all tenants have opportunities to make their views known. Good understanding of the use of digital engagement Identifying service improvements from tenants’ feedback and for updating tenants on how their views have made a difference. Assisting with evidencing compliance with regulatory standards by maintaining accurate records, evidence and performance information relating to tenant engagement and influence. We would prefer a minimum of one year working with customers in a Housing or public service organisation, preferably with an understanding of the regulatory requirements of the registered provider of social housing. However we would welcome applications from anyone with transferable skills. About You We are looking for exceptional candidates with a range of skills, including: Great interpersonal skills with the ability to develop and maintain positive, collaborative relationships with residents, colleagues and stakeholders Skilled at using surveys, focus groups and other methods to gain meaningful feedback and customer insight Good understanding best practice and the use of digital engagement methods and of good practice in increasing digital inclusion Minimum of one year working with customers in a Housing or public service organisation, Closing Date : 20 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates 29 Jul 2026 Application Details Salary Please note that Runnymede Borough Council is currently undertaking a pay and grading review, which is expected to be implemented during the summer. The successful applicant will be appointed under the Council’s current grading and salary structure and, following implementation of the new framework, will be assimilated into the revised pay structure in line with all existing employees. Please see the advert on our website for the full details of this role including the job description and person specification. To apply please use the link: https://jobs.runnymede.gov.uk/vacancies/156/housing-engagement-and-inclusion-officer.html Website : https://www.runnymede.gov.uk/
item.dc:creator: Runnymede Borough Council

item.title: Constituency Support Manager
item.description: This is a senior role within the MP's constituency team. We're looking for an experienced, proactive individual with a proven track record of leading projects, building partnerships and delivering successful campaigns. The successful candidate will help shape the MP's engagement across Blackpool South, leading community initiatives, supporting strategic communications and ensuring a strong local presence. Key Responsibilities * Lead community engagement, building relationships with residents, community groups, charities, businesses and key stakeholders. * Develop and maintain a constituency stakeholder and community engagement map. * Lead constituency campaigns, projects and community initiatives from planning to delivery. * Coordinate weekly Street Surgeries and the MP's regular "Chat with Chris" events. * Plan and coordinate constituency literature distribution * Produce and edit digital content, including graphics, photography and video * Provide communications support during constituency visits and events * Draft report backs, email newsletters and the Annual Report. * Build productive relationships with local organisations and research issues affecting Blackpool South. * Occasional evening and weekend working required. Essential Skills and Experience * Significant experience in a senior communications, campaigns, community engagement or project management role. * Prior experience of organising and delivering campaigns and/or community engagement projects is essential. * Proven ability to manage multiple projects and deliver successful outcomes. * Excellent organisational, written and verbal communication skills. * Experience delivering communications across digital and social media platforms. * Experience organising public events and stakeholder engagement activities. * Self-motivated, able to work independently and exercise sound judgement. * Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to build relationships with a wide range of stakeholders. * High levels of political awareness, discretion and professionalism. Desirable Skills and Personal Qualities * Experience in Parliament, local government, political campaigns, charities or a similar public-facing organisation. * Strategic, creative and solutions-focused. * Passionate about public service and committed to delivering excellent support for constituents. * Sympathetic to the aims and values of the Labour Party. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 31 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews to be held in Blackpool South in early August. Application Details Please send a CV (no more than two sides A4) and a cover letter (no more than one side A4) to chris.webb.mp@parliament.uk with the subject line "Constituency Support Manager" by 11.59pm Website : http://www.ChrisWebb.org
item.dc:creator: Chris Webb MP (Blackpool South)

item.title: Executive Assistant
item.description: Apsana Begum MP is looking to recruit an Executive Assistant with experience of diary and project management, for a time-limited period. The postholder will be required to work outside of normal working hours and primarily between Sundays and Thursdays. Key responsibilities Project management Resource and project management responsibilities for e.g. conceptualising itineries, organising and coordinating travel and meetings. Managing budgets and providing administrative management in relation to MP’s expenses scheme via IPSA Online for e.g. managing expenses, producing expense reports & submissions. Compilation of briefing packs based on working knowledge of relevant legislation and parliamentary business. Executive support Providing executive support to the MP and interest groups as required for e.g. complex diary coordination. Proficient use Microsoft Office including Outlook, Teams, Excel & SharePoint. Ensuring all enquiries are dealt with in a timely manner. Holding and processing data and information confidentially, in line with GDPR Minute-taking. Stakeholder liaison and management Engaging professionally, confidentially and diplomatically with senior level stakeholders through a variety of methods and maintaining positive ongoing relationships. Liaising with high-level interest groups and representatives including people from diverse backgrounds and without English as a first language. Dealing with complex queries, complaints & protocols, including drafting letters. Characteristics, Skills and Experience Essential Proficient use Microsoft Office including Outlook, Teams, Excel & SharePoint Experience of providing executive support and undertaking project management Working outside of normal working hours Committed to the political values of Apsana Begum MP Desirable Bangla language skills Experience of working across time zones Key Competencies Planning and Organising Following Policies and Procedures Delivering Results and Meeting Customer Expectations Relating and Networking Applying Expertise Writing and Reporting Working with People Learning and Researching Please note that this role will be full-time or part-time depending on the start date agreed with the successful candidate, due to this role only being required for a time-limited period. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 21 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will begin to be held on the Thursday 23rd July or Thursday 30th July and will involve written tests. Please be advised that only applicants available on the interview dates should apply and the deadline may close earlier should a sufficient number of applications be received. Application Details Applicants should submit a CV (of no more than 2 pages) and a Written Statement (of no more than 2 pages) demonstrating skills, knowledge and experience in the following key areas: Independent decision-making Self-management Complex diary and project management Communicating with tact and diplomacy and maintaining confidentiality to apsana.begum.mp@parliament.uk with the subject line ‘Executive Assistant 2026 – Full name’. Website : http://www.apsanabegum.com
item.dc:creator: Apsana Begum MP (Poplar and Limehouse)

item.title: Communications & Public Affairs Associate
item.description: The Environmental Services Association (ESA) - the representative body for the recycling and waste industry - is recruiting a full-time Communications & Public Affairs Associate. This role represents an opportunity for a highly motivated individual to join a London-based team shaping policy on a high-profile environmental issue, influencing government, legislators, the regulator and other external stakeholders. Key responsibilities 1. Reporting to the Executive Director, help to promote the UK’s recycling and waste industry. 2. Monitor political, economic, legislative and regulatory developments relevant to the industry. Coordinate and draft responses to media and other queries raised by journalists, government and other external bodies. 3. Support the Association’s public affairs objectives by identifying stakeholders (government officials, Parliamentarians and other external interested parties), drafting briefings and coordinating meetings. 4. Support ESA’s communications lead to align the Association’s public affairs objectives with external campaigns, including the organisation of events for external audiences. 5. Co-ordinate ESA’s Strategic Communications & Public Affairs Group of Member Company experts. 6. Undertake communications and public affairs reporting, including channel performance, media monitoring and political engagement logs 7. Establish strong relationships with key stakeholders, including politicians, government officials, green NGOs + other relevant organisations. 8. Manage relationships with key technical contacts within ESA’s Membership 9. Help support the rest of the policy team across all areas of ESA’s work Required skills and qualifications • Minimum degree level or equivalent, in any discipline, from a leading institution • Keen interest in policy development and environmental/recycling and waste policy in particular • Ability to think laterally • Good negotiator + oral communicator • Excellent at developing and maintaining relationships with a wide range of people from different backgrounds and at different job seniorities • Proficient in the use of basic business software packages (Microsoft Office) Closing Date : 7 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews to take place in August and potentially September. ESA will be flexible to accommodate the right candidates. Role to start as soon as possible thereafter. Application Details Interested candidates should email their CV and a short covering letter to recruitment@esauk.org Website : http://www.esauk.org
item.dc:creator: Environmental Services Association

item.title: Policy Advisor
item.description: An exciting opportunity has arisen for a Policy Advisor to join our dynamic and well-respected Policy, Public Affairs and Campaigns Team. We want to ensure people affected by dementia benefit from progress in research, by providing evidence and insights to influence decision-makers. This ranges from making the case to increase investment in dementia research, embedding the concept of brain health with key decision makers, supporting new ways to diagnose the diseases that cause dementia, through to preparing health systems to be ready for future dementia treatments. We work closely with key stakeholders, including government and other decision makers, to shape policy on dementia prevention, research and access to future treatments. At a historic time in the field, with the first drugs that tackle Alzheimer’s becoming available, this is a fantastic opportunity for the post-holder to develop specialist knowledge, working closely with the Policy Manager, and to contribute to significant projects that can change the future of dementia. We are offering a permanent, full-time position with a focus on the dementia research environment and regulation, including health technology appraisals. Closing Date : 26 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will be arranged once shortlisting has been completed. Please indicate in your covering letter if you are unable to attend an interview on a certain date. Application Details https://alzheimersresearchuk.current-vacancies.com/Jobs/Advert/4252617?cid=3266&t=Policy-Advisor Website : http://alzheimersresearchuk.com
item.dc:creator: Alzheimers Research UK

item.title: Support Officer (Visits and Policy)
item.description: Alex is looking for an organised and resilient self-starter with top-notch political acumen to join her team. A key focus will be arranging up to 100 constituency engagements annually. This is much more than diary management, although it is your job to make sure your MP arrives on time and well briefed! It’s about building relationships with stakeholders; horizon scanning for key issues in Westminster and the constituency; having great ideas; and knowing what resonates in the news. You’ll need to use charm and persuasion, picking up the phone to secure an invite, or having to gently say no, with no hard feelings. You will write well. One day we will suddenly need a briefing at pace. Another day you’ll be drafting a column for a newspaper, or bullet points for a speech, or replying to constituents. You will adapt your style accordingly. You’ll make connections, following up an issue you heard about at a surgery with a Parliamentary question; or hearing about a new law in a debate that people in the constituency need to know about. There will also be day-to-day Parliamentary work to carry out such as applying for debates, booking tours and compiling briefing packs. And as we’re a small, fast-paced team, there will be times when everyone has to pitch in and support each other with a variety of tasks across the board. About you: A great organiser Confident communicator Strong political judgement Positive, self-motivated and creative Adaptable and resilient Fast paced Committed to Labour Location is flexible with some travel required to the Westminster and Leighton Buzzard offices. If you require any adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know. We are open to term time only working. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 20 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews w/c 27 July Start date September 2026 Application Details Complete the online form at https://forms.gle/pfhANULgKWB8ZxC2A Website : http://www.alexmayer.co.uk
item.dc:creator: Alex Mayer MP (Dunstable and Leighton Buzzard)

item.title: Digital Content Creator
item.description: Sarah Jones MP is looking for a creative, proactive and detail-oriented Digital Content Creator to suggest, create and share compelling and engaging content for local residents. This is an exciting opportunity to join a dedicated team serving local people in Croydon West, supporting an active and impactful MP. Key Responsibilities: • Deliver end-to-end high quality, high engagement, video content to promote the MP’s work and campaigns across the constituency– including content ideation, and filming and editing short-form and long-form pieces tailored for a range of channels, including Instagram, Facebook, X, and TikTok • Accompany the MP to visits and events across the constituency, capturing and creating compelling content • Produce visual assets for social media using software such as Canva • Proactively lead on digital strategy, including planning, creating and developing engaging content across a wide range of channels • Edit and subtitle video clips using tools such as TikTok and Capcut • Suggest content ideas, formats and trends to grow reach and engagement Essential Skills and Experience: • Strong skills and experience in digital content creation and delivery • ⁠Ability to spot trends and react quickly to news. Strong organisational and time management abilities • Ability to work independently, manage competing priorities, and use initiative, with good attention to detail • Analytics (understanding what content performs and why) • Ability to translate policy into clear visual stories Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 31 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates September start date Application Details Please send a CV of no more than two pages and a one-page cover letter. Please also share links to three examples of videos you’ve created previously. Send these to sarah.jones.mp@parliament.uk with ‘[Your First and Last Name]: Digital Content Creator Application’ in the subject heading. In the cover letter, please explain how your skills and experience evidences the requirements set out in the role description. Applications which are not tailored for this specific role will not be considered. Website : http://www.sarah-jones.org/
item.dc:creator: Sarah Jones MP (Croydon West)

item.title: Communications Manager
item.description: Key Responsibilities Develop and issue press statements and releases to the media Build and maintain strong working relationships with journalists, editors, and media outlets Plan content and manage the SDLP communications grid Deliver strategic communications, including identifying and securing opportunities to profile SDLP representatives in the media Communicate the SDLP's core messages consistently and effectively across all media engagement Prepare and support elected representatives ahead of media appearances Monitor media and political developments, anticipate challenges, and provide proactive advice to representatives and the wider team Manage day-to-day media enquiries, ensuring timely and accurate responses Compile briefing packs, including media briefings, and assist with speechwriting Contribute to the development of campaigns to promote policy proposals and party priorities Person Specification Essential Criteria At least 3 years' experience in journalism or working directly with the media Proven experience developing and issuing press statements and releases Experience delivering strategic communications , including identifying opportunities to profile representatives or spokespeople in the media Strong writing skills , with the ability to communicate core messages clearly, accurately, and persuasively under time pressure Experience in crisis communications and reputation management Established contacts and credibility with journalists and media outlets A strong understanding of the Northern Ireland and wider media landscape , including print, broadcast, and online Ability to work flexibly , including regular out-of-hours availability to respond to breaking news and media demands A strong understanding of current affairs and the political environment in Northern Ireland , and a strong awareness of the SDLP's policies and values Desirable Criteria Experience working in-house in a political party, public affairs, or campaigning organisation Experience preparing briefing materials and lines to take for elected representatives Experience using social media to support media and communications activity Education & Previous Roles A degree-level qualification , or a minimum of 5 years relevant experience in journalism, media relations, press or communications work, or a closely related field. Closing Date : 22 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will take place during the week commencing Monday 27 July . Application Details Full details of the role and the Application Form are available at: www.sdlp.ie/communications_manager To apply, complete the Application Form and email it to headquarters@sdlp.ie by 12:00pm on the closing date , using the subject line: 'Communications Manager Job Application' . Website : http://www.sdlp.ie
