item.title: PhD Scholarships in Global History and Governance
item.description: Scuola Superiore Meridionale - Doctoral Programs – 42 th cycle – Academic year 2026/2027 Call for Applications APPLICATION DEADLINE 27 AUGUST 2026 The Scuola Superiore Meridionale (SSM) hereby announces a call for applications for PhD scholarships for the academic year 2026-2027 in the Doctoral Programs in Global History and Governance The program will award 5 scholarships for a duration of four-years. Courses will begin on November 2, 2026. No fees or contributions are required to enroll; scholarships will be disbursed monthly and additional research funding made available each year. Applications will only be accepted if submitted through our website: here Applications received in any other form will not be considered. The SSM is not responsible for any online application not properly completed by candidates. The deadline for completing the online application is 27 August 2026 at 14:00 CET . The deadline for the submission of reference letters is 31 August 2026 at 17:00 CET . Candidates intending to apply for more than one PhD program can do so by submitting multiple applications – a separate one for each program they intend to apply. Accepted candidates will be required to enroll by October 31, 2026. More details on the admission requirements, the selection process, the enrollment procedures are made available here . For anything that is not specified here or in attachments, please refer to the official SSM website: here . Only the full Italian-language call for applications is binding in case of controversies.
item.dc:creator: Scuola Superiore Meridionale

item.title: Caseworker
item.description: A fixed-term Constituency Caseworker role to cover maternity leave is available within my team, supporting local people and helping resolve the issues that matter to them. This position sits at the heart of community support. The Caseworker will be, as part of a team, a first point of contact for constituents who need help with a wide range of concerns, including housing, benefits, health, education, immigration and local services. The role involves managing a caseload, communicating with government departments and agencies, and providing clear and accurate advice. The location of the role is flexible. There will also be opportunities to visit the Houses of Parliament. I am looking for someone who is compassionate, organised and committed to helping others. Strong communication, empathy and listening skills are essential, along with attention to detail and the ability to work calmly under pressure. This role suits someone who enjoys variety and is keen to learn. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 31 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews in August Start date: On or around 1 September 2026 Application Details How to Apply: Please submit your CV, a covering letter and contact details for two referees to julia.buckley.mp@parliament.uk (references will only be taken with permission). Please clearly indicate in your covering letter your preferred days and location for work. Website : https://shrewsburylabour.org.uk/julia-buckley/
item.dc:creator: Julia Buckley MP (Shrewsbury)

item.title: Strategic Communications Manager (Public Affairs)
item.description: Are you a dog-loving strategic communications professional with experience planning captivating public affairs campaigns? We’re looking for a Strategic Communications Manager to develop and deliver impactful marketing communications strategies and campaigns, with a particular focus on our public affairs activity. What does this role do? As Strategic Communications Manager, you’ll: build strategic plans for key projects, scoping, and planning for communications across the marketing mix (paid, owned and earned channels), focusing on public affairs projects, as we strive to influence governments and engage the general public to improve animal welfare, shape key messages for campaigns, often distilling complex information into clear narratives, using evidence-based messaging frameworks, brief content delivery teams and channel leads on activity, closely monitoring to ensure assets and output are on time and to spec, embed evaluation frameworks from the very beginning of every campaign, ensuring success can be measured effectively. Could this be you? To be successful in this role, you’ll have experience of leading strategic marketing and communications projects, utilising excellent project management and communication skills to deliver and shape compelling stories and campaigns. We’re particularly interested in hearing from candidates who’ve worked in the public affairs space previously, with the ability to engage both mass audiences and political audiences with our campaigns and public affairs messages. You’ll have excellent stakeholder management experience, as well as strong evaluation skills, with experience of using clear evaluation methodologies. A commitment to Dogs Trust and the work we do is essential. About Dogs Trust We love dogs. That’s why we do whatever we can to make sure every four-legged friend gets the love they deserve. We’ll never put a healthy dog down, so our work is focused on helping dogs in need, supporting owners every step of the walk, and creating a better world for dogs in the future. It’s what we’ve been doing since 1891 and how we’ve grown to become the UK’s leading dog charity, helping 12,000 loyal friends find their forever homes every year. Closing Date : 19 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews for this role are provisionally scheduled for week commencing 27th July 2026 and will take place on Teams. Application Details To apply for this position please visit this link: https://careers.dogstrust.org.uk/en/postings/4ed1b8a5-315b-4ebe-b727-538f8bed3bd7 . Our application process requires you submit a personal statement explaining your interest and suitability for the role. Website : http://www.dogstrust.org.uk/
item.dc:creator: Dogs Trust

item.title: Deputy Director, Policy
item.description: The Deputy Director will be responsible for leading, motivating and developing a high performing team of policy professionals and support staff. This role is part of the senior management team in the Policy Group within UUK. UUK would positively consider applications from civil or public servants who would wish to apply in the form of a secondment. The purpose of this role is to ensure UUK provides policy leadership and maximises its influence on the higher education policy agenda on behalf of the sector and in support of the organisation’s Strategic Plan. In addition to leading policy development across a range of areas this role will require building and maintaining strong and influential senior level relationships and networks within the sector, government and other influential stakeholders. Closing Date : 19 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews are expected to take place on Wednesday 29 July 2026. Application Details https://www.uukjobs.co.uk/UUK02026 Website : https://www.universitiesuk.ac.uk/
item.dc:creator: Universities UK

item.title: Client executive
item.description: Lauder Teacher is a specialist strategic advisory firm operating at the intersection of real estate, infrastructure, technology and capital markets. We advise ambitious private companies, global investors and listed businesses on reputation, growth and influence. Our work spans strategic communications, investor relations, market intelligence, public affairs, executive profiling and broadcast media. We also co-produce one of the sector's leading podcasts with Property Week, featuring many of the biggest names shaping the built environment. This is an excellent opportunity for someone at the start of their career who is curious about business, economics, politics and the forces shaping cities and the global economy.You'll support senior advisers on a wide range of client projects, from researching investment trends and analysing policy to preparing briefing notes, producing reports and monitoring developments affecting markets. One day you could be researching interest rates and real estate investment, the next preparing a briefing for a FTSE chief executive or analysing a major corporate announcement. You'll work directly with experienced advisers on high-profile assignments that most graduates wouldn't encounter early in their careers. Previous property experience isn't essential. Curiosity, analytical ability and a genuine interest in business matter far more. Key responsibilities Analyse industry trends and produce reports. Monitor media, political and regulatory developments. Support strategic communications, investor relations and research projects. Prepare presentations, reports and client briefings. Assist with podcasts, events and thought leadership. Support project coordination and client delivery. Use data and digital tools to identify trends. Person specification Strong interest in business, economics, politics and current affairs. Excellent analytical, research and writing skills. Curious, organised and detail-oriented. Comfortable using data, spreadsheets and research tools. Positive, collaborative and proactive. Strong Microsoft Office and Google Workspace skills. A degree is welcomed but not essential. Intelligence, curiosity and ambition matter most. Closing Date : 31 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interview will start as soon as possible. Start date - to be discussed (Likely end of August/Beginning of September) Application Details Please send your CV alongside with your cover letter to claire@lauderteacher.com Website : http://www.lauderteacher.com
item.dc:creator: Lauder Teacher Associates ltd

item.title: Parliamentary Officer
item.description: Green MP Hannah Spencer is looking for additional capacity in her team to help her deliver for constituents locally and in Parliament. Responsible for helping across both the local and Westminster teams, you will be someone who thrives on having lots to do, has excellent organisational and communication skills, can adapt to changing plans and remains calm under pressure. You should have a strong understanding of the main issues facing constituents and a commitment to making sure Hannah is responsive, effective and proactive in her work with residents and stakeholders. Hannah is keen to receive applications from diverse backgrounds and especially from candidates that are not on a traditional pathway into working in politics. Open to flexible part-time applications. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 7 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews to take place during the week of 31st August with a start date as soon as is practical afterwards. Application Details Please see https://greenparty.org.uk/get-involved/work-for-us/parliamentary-officer-hannah-spencer-mp/ for more details and a link to the job application platform for this role Website : https://members.parliament.uk/member/5447/contact
item.dc:creator: Hannah Spencer MP (Gorton and Denton)

item.title: Caseworker
item.description: About the role Tom Morrison, Lib Dem MP for Cheadle, is looking for an organised, empathetic and resilient Caseworker to join his constituency team. This is a varied and rewarding role at the heart of Cheadle's community, helping local residents resolve serious problems with government departments, local services and other organisations. You will manage a busy and sensitive caseload covering issues working directly with constituents to understand their situation and pursue the best possible outcome on their behalf. You will be the first point of contact for many residents at what is often a difficult time in their lives, so a calm, professional and compassionate manner is essential. Strong writing, communication, IT and organisational skills are essential, along with an understanding of how to use AI tools responsibly to support casework and correspondence. Key responsibilities ● Manage a full and varied caseload from initial enquiry through to resolution, keeping accurate records on the office's case management system ● Correspond with constituents by letter, email and phone, explaining next steps clearly and sensitively ● Liaise with government departments and agencies to progress cases ● Draft letters to ministers, public bodies and organisations on behalf of the MP ● Identify cases that may require a wider campaign, media interest or a parliamentary question, and flag these to the MP and Communications Manager ● Run and support regular constituency surgeries, casework outreach events and constituency meetings ● Maintain strict confidentiality and handle sensitive personal data in line with data protection requirements ● Provide general administrative support to the constituency office as required Person specification and full advert can be found here - https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Cezg90Rr55urXi8I97bHqfnJ9h5dwHadx6Po6CZWgdk/edit?usp=sharing Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 24 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interview Process Work-based task set 28 Jul (1 week to complete). Video interviews: notified 4 Aug, held w/c 10 Aug. Final in-person interviews with the MP w/c 17 Aug. Application Details How to apply Please send a CV and a one page covering letter, setting out how you meet the person specification, to Mark Jones, Office Manager, at mark.jones@parliament.uk , with "Caseworker Application" in the subject line. If you have any questions about the role in advance of applying, please contact Mark at mark.jones@parliament.uk Tom Morrison MP is committed to equality of opportunity and welcomes applications from all sections of the community. Website : http://www.cheadle-libdems.org.uk
item.dc:creator: Tom Morrison MP (Cheadle)

item.title: Policy Officer
item.description: Department: Policy & Strategy Hours: 37 per week Location: Home Based (UK wide travel as required) The Strategy and Policy Team is part of the National Fire Chiefs Council (NFCC) Strategy and Corporate Services Directorate. The successful candidate will play a key role within the team, which incorporates policy and strategy support across Fire and Rescue Service functions. The Strategy and Policy Team work closely with subject matter experts, insight analysts, and communications colleagues across NFCC to support and produce policy advice, positions, and content. Through the timely and clear production of evidence-based advice, our primary objective is to support national policy development, with the aim of improving the safety of the communities we serve. What you will be doing The successful candidate will report to the Senior Policy Manager and be responsible for supporting the development of policy advice, positions, and responses to Government across a range of topics, with a focus on FRSs’ protection and building safety activity. The post holder will be part of a team producing submissions, advice, consultation responses, position statements and a range of other products for UKFRS across areas of FRS duties. Responsibilities will include engagement with UK FRSs to ensure that policies and national positions under development enjoy the support of our members and appropriately reflect their views. In addition to working with other members of the Strategy and Policy Team, you will also work with technical, communications, data, and governance colleagues from other NFCC teams. The successful candidate will also attend meetings and engage with Government Departments. The post holder may also be involved with the preparation of research reports, impact assessments, cost benefit analysis of policy options, equality impact assessments, and presentation of data where relevant. Who we are looking for We are looking for someone with strong writing and analysis skills, with the ability to synthesise large amounts of technical information and translate that into products that are easy for a non-technical audience to grasp. You will bring a positive approach to being part of a team. Experience of working in a fast-paced and political environment, and of handling high profile issues is desirable. Closing Date : 24 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates PLEASE NOTE – THIS VACANCY WILL CLOSE BEFORE THE CLOSING DATE IF WE RECEIVE A HIGH NUMBER OF APPLICATIONS. Application Details Full details, along with the job description and the link to apply via our online application form, can be found on our website here: https://nfcc.org.uk/fire-sector-jobs/policy-officer-nfcc-3/ Please note, we will not accept CV's for this role. Website : https://nfcc.org.uk/
item.dc:creator: National Fire Chiefs Council Limited

item.title: Senior Caseworker
item.description: Sureena Brackenridge MP is seeking a highly motivated, committed and compassionate individual to join her constituency office as a Senior Caseworker . This key role will provide high-quality casework support to constituents, managing complex enquiries and ensuring effective resolution of a wide range of issues. The successful candidate will deliver excellent constituent service, work closely with the MP and wider office team, and support colleagues to advocate on behalf of residents working in a fast-paced environment. Responsibilities will include: • Managing a complex and varied caseload, providing advice, support and advocacy to constituents on a wide range of issues • Attending constituency surgeries, meetings and outreach activities to engage with residents • Taking ownership of cases from enquiry through to resolution, ensuring actions are progressed promptly and records maintained • Liaising with Government departments, Wolverhampton and Walsall Councils, public bodies, third sector organisations and other stakeholders to resolve enquiries effectively • Identifying trends within casework and providing insight to support MP’s priorities • Drafting professional correspondence, including letters, emails, briefings and documents on behalf of the MP • Maintaining accurate and confidential records in line with GDPR, the Data Protection Act and parliamentary procedures Essential skills: • Excellent communication skills, with the ability to engage sensitively with a diverse range of constituents • Strong organisational skills and the ability to manage competing priorities, complex cases and deadlines • Excellent IT skills, including experience using casework management systems (the office uses Caseworker), Microsoft Office applications and online systems • Ability to draft clear, accurate and professional correspondence • Experience of managing casework and working with public services, advice agencies or similar organisations. Previous experience of working in an MP’s office is preferred • Ability to exercise sound judgement and contribute positively as part of a small team Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 24 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Closing time: 4:00 pm Interview date W/C 27th July 2026 Starting date subject to security clearance, references from previous employment and with consideration of any notice period. Application Details Application Details Please send your CV (no more than 2 sides of A4) and covering letter (no more than one side of A4) setting out how you meet the essential and desirable criteria to sureena.brackenridge.mp@parliament.uk Website : http://www.sureenabrackenridge.com/
item.dc:creator: Sureena Brackenridge MP (Wolverhampton North East)

item.title: Office Manager
item.description: Location: Westminster, London (some hybrid working available) Hours: 0.5 FTE Contract: Permanent The Role We are seeking an organised, proactive and dependable Office Manager to help ensure the smooth running of the Constitution Society. Working closely with the executive team, you will play a central role in the day-to-day administration of the charity. This is a varied position combining office management, executive support, governance administration, financial administration, communications and event management. The successful candidate will be highly organised, comfortable working independently and able to manage a varied workload within a small, friendly organisation. Key Responsibilities Office and Operations • Manage the day-to-day operation of the Society's Westminster office. • Maintain office systems, filing and organisational records. • Identify opportunities to improve office procedures and administrative systems. Executive and Governance Support • Provide executive support to the Directors, including diary management, meeting scheduling and travel arrangements. • Assisting in managing Society correspondence and general enquiries. • Organising trustee meetings, staff meetings and the Annual General Meeting, including preparing and circulating papers and minutes. Finance Administration • Process invoices, expenses and purchase orders. • Maintain financial records and reconcile routine expenditure. • Administer contractor payments. Person Specification Essential • At least two years' experience in an office management, executive assistant, operations or senior administrative role. • Excellent organisational skills with exceptional attention to detail. • Experience of financial administration, including processing invoices and maintaining financial records. Desirable • Experience working in the charity, education, public policy or not-for-profit sectors. • Experience supporting trustee boards or senior leadership teams. • Experience organising public events or conferences. For full details see: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1ALoXf6iG85e3PMD5AH2JRqrhUKif7RzTQyiVLXmyU9A/edit Closing Date : 31 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will be held in Westminster week commencing 10 August 2026. Application Details How to Apply Please submit your application via the googleform at: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfwc9Xgapt6LRzw_I5r0ihtFdnPaxxlPb0ynFNliauzCTvkxA/viewform?usp=header Please upload a combined cover letter (one page maximum), CV (two pages maximum). These should be uploaded in a single PDF or Word document. Use your full name as the file title: i.e. "John Smith". Website : http://consoc.org.uk

item.title: Programme Manager - Artificial Intelligence (12 month FTC)
item.description: Role purpose This new role offers an exciting opportunity to join our fast-paced Tech and Innovation programme that leads techUK’s activity around a range of emerging technologies including AI, Robotics, Quantum, Digital ID and more. The Programme Manager for AI will help to deliver techUK’s programme of activity and engagement focused on accelerating AI adoption and deployment across all UK industries and sectors. Working closely with techUK colleagues, members, key stakeholders, and the wider technology sector the Programme Manager will bring new and creative ideas to techUK’s AI adoption work and support the execution of current and future activities specifically techUK’s recently created AI Adoption Coalition. The main deliverable for this role will be working closely with the Director of Technology and Innovation to deliver activity and outputs focused on accelerating the adoption of responsible AI that add value for techUK’s members and stakeholders. This will include organising member meetings and roundtables, large scale events (including techUK’s annual AI conference and AI Adoption Coalition events) and other profile raising activities that showcase the opportunities of AI adoption. A central focus for this role is to work to ensure that techUK’s AI content, activity, strategy, and other outputs focused on AI adoption are aligned with members needs and wants, as well as the broader AI adoption debate and discussion to ensure techUK’s work in this area remains relevant and credible. This role requires an individual who has initiative, enthusiasm, is organised, can prioritise effectively, is forward thinking, can build strong connections and relationships with a variety of stakeholders, work independently and as part of a team, is willing to bring new ideas and suggestions to the AI programme and can excel in an exciting and fast-paced environment spanning a range of projects and techUK teams. The role is not technical and it would best suit an individual with a strong interest in technology policy, strategy, or ecosystems. Closing Date : 31 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates A full version of the job role can be found on our website linked below. Candidates who are shortlisted will be contacted promptly to arrange an interview. Application Details Apply for the role by clicking on the below link and submitting your CV for consideration: https://careers.techuk.org/postings/86c91cde-6aac-4a23-9fed-2a65ba05f2d4?utm_medium=job_board&utm_source=W4MP Website : https://techuk.org
item.dc:creator: techUK

item.title: Programme Manager - International Trade
item.description: Role Overview techUK is looking for an energetic, commercially-minded Programme Manager to grow our international trade programme, helping UK tech companies find opportunities overseas through trade missions, delegations, and a strong events programme. This is an outward-facing role blending member engagement, business development, and stakeholder management — ideal for someone who enjoys building networks and making things happen rather than a policy specialist. No two weeks look the same, from leading trade show delegations to hosting ministerial roundtables and connecting with prospective members. Role Purpose The Programme Manager will help deliver techUK's international trade programme by supporting members expand internationally, building strategic relationships across government and industry, delivering high-quality events and delegations, and contributing to the programme's commercial growth. The role will also play an important part in ensuring members understand and benefit from the UK's international trade agenda, including Free Trade Agreements and wider international market opportunities. Key Responsibilities: Programme Delivery Member Engagement International Trade & Partnerships Commercial delivery Communications A full version of the job role can be found on our website linked below. Closing Date : 4 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates Candidates who are shortlisted will be contacted promptly to arrange an interview. Application Details Apply for the role by clicking on the below link and submitting your CV for consideration: https://careers.techuk.org/postings/255bffe5-4d6c-4d9d-b8c8-2dc72f73a220?utm_medium=job_board&utm_source=W4MP Website : https://techuk.org

item.title: Constituency Assistant
item.description: Gregor Poynton MP is seeking a motivated, organised and community-focused Constituency Assistant to join his Livingston constituency office. This is a varied and rewarding role supporting constituents while helping to deliver effective communications and community engagement. The successful candidate will play an important part in providing a high-quality service, supporting the MP's casework, social media, media relations and public events, and responding to the issues that matter most to local residents. Key responsibilities: Analyse patterns of enquiries and produce reports. Assist at surgeries, constituency meetings and other events, following up as required. Deal with constituent enquiries by phone, email, social media and in person, including more complex cases where appropriate. Draft responses to constituents. Gather relevant information and maintain accurate, confidential case records in line with data protection legislation. Log and monitor cases, ensuring actions are completed promptly, sensitively and accurately. Manage and develop the constituency's social media presence, producing graphics, videos and content promoting news, campaigns and surgeries. Respond to social media enquiries and comments. Monitor media coverage and prepare press releases and other communications on constituency matters. Liaise with local media and meet publication deadlines. Support the MP at regular street surgeries, coffee mornings and other constituency engagement events. Work occasional evenings or weekends and travel within the constituency when required. Desirable (not essential): A full UK driving licence and access to a car. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 9 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will be held online during the week commencing 10 August 2026. The successful candidate will be expected to start on Monday 7 September 2026. We reserve the right to interview and appoint before the closing date. Application Details Applicants should submit a CV and a covering letter (maximum one page) outlining their suitability for the role, clearly demonstrating how they meet the requirements set out in the job description. Applications should be sent by email to gregor.poynton.mp@parliament.uk with the subject line: Constituency Assistant Application – First Name Surname. If you have any questions about the role, please contact the office using the same email address. Due to the anticipated volume of applications, only candidates shortlisted for interview will be contacted. Website : http://www.gregorpoynton.com
item.dc:creator: Gregor Poynton MP (Livingston)

item.title: Account Executive - Health Policy and Public Affairs
item.description: Hinton Advisers is seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Account Executive to join our growing team in September 2026. You’ll work across the UK and global health policy landscape, supporting clients to design and deliver public and government affairs programmes. Hinton Advisers is a small independent agency offering specialist health policy and public affairs advice to clients across the life science ecosystem. This is an exciting opportunity to be part of a dynamic team and build your career with an agency that’s investing in its people from the start. Full details on the role - what we are offer and what we are looking for - are avilable on our website: https://hintonadvisers.com/job/account-executive-health-policy-government-affairs/ Closing Date : 14 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates Deadline:17:00 GMT. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, and we may close the process early if we find the right candidate - so we encourage early submissions. Start date: September 2026 Application Details At Hinton Advisers, we are committed to creating an inclusive workplace and we welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, experiences and perspectives. To apply, please send your CV and a cover letter of no more than one page outlining your experience and why you’re the right fit for this role. In your cover letter, we’d also like you to outline one health policy issue / area that you’d like to work on (either within the UK or at a global level). Please send CV and cover letter to careers@hintonadvisers.com . Website : http://www.hintonadvisers.com

item.title: Constituency Assistant (3x Part-Time roles)
item.description: Hours: 0.6 FTE (22.5 hours per week). Working pattern: Office hours, with one in five weekends on call. Flexible working can be agreed, but availability most Fridays is essential. About the role Do you want to make a real difference in Sunderland? Lewis Atkinson MP is recruiting three Constituency Assistants to strengthen relationships across Sunderland Central, engage residents with their MP and support work to improve local neighbourhoods. You'll build relationships with residents, businesses, charities, community groups and public services, supporting Lewis' commitment to being visible, accessible and responsive. We're looking for people with energy, initiative and a passion for bringing people together who enjoy working in the community and helping solve local problems. Key responsibilities Build relationships with residents, businesses, charities and community groups. Identify local issues and help coordinate responses. Organise community events, meetings and engagement activities. Represent Lewis at meetings and events. Work with local partners to resolve issues. Support resident communications, including letters and social media. Identify opportunities to strengthen engagement. Work with the wider team to ensure community concerns are acted upon. About you You'll be proactive, approachable and organised, with strong communication skills and a passion for community engagement. Essential: Experience organising community activity or engagement. Experience in the charity, voluntary or community sector (paid or voluntary). Strong relationship-building and organisational skills. Excellent written and verbal communication. Confidence representing Lewis professionally. Good IT skills. Ability to manage competing priorities and work independently. Understanding of the role of an MP and local government. Sympathy with the aims and values of Lewis Atkinson MP. Desirable: Knowledge of Sunderland Central. Political, campaigning or public affairs experience. Social media or content creation experience. No two days are the same. You could be visiting a local charity, organising a community event, responding to a local issue or helping residents connect with their MP. This is a rewarding opportunity for someone who wants to make a tangible difference in Sunderland. If that's you, we'd love to hear from you. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 7 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates We are looking for people who can make an immediate impact in this role. Interviews will be carried out on a rolling basis, prompt applications are therefore encouraged. Application Details To apply please send a copy of an up to date CV along with a covering letter detailing your suitability for the role to lewis.atkinson.mp@parliament.uk Should you wish to speak with a member of the team before submitting an applciation please call 0191 565 5327 and ask to speak with Matthew. Website : https://members.parliament.uk/member/5028/contact

item.title: Caseworker
item.description: • You will engage with constituents confidentially, via telephone enquiries, emails, face to face and social media. • You will manage individual cases ensuring they are logged and progressed with all identified actions taken. • You will keep all information and records confidential and in line with data protection legislation. • You will assist with surgeries and other meetings and follow up action as appropriate. • You will be responsible for gathering relevant information and/or evidence to progress cases accordingly. • You will be expected to liaise with NGO’s, Government, Local Authorities and the voluntary sector as part of your day to day responsibilities. • As part of a team you will be managing a large caseload of often complex, and sometimes distressing casework that you will undertake in a caring and sensitive manner. • You may be asked to reply to enquiries about Government Policy • On occasion you will be expected to research and then brief the MP on any number of topics, including analysing patterns of enquiries using the relevant software. • There may be a requirement for the role holder to work outside of normal hours and travel for work occasionally. Key Skills Required • Shares the aims and values of the Labour Party • Attention to detail • Ability to work under pressure to fixed deadlines • Excellent written and oral communications skills • Ability to work in a team in a collaborative manner • Excellent organisational skills • Experience of Microsoft tools including Word, Exel and Outlook. Key Skills Desirable • Previous experience of carrying out casework either working in an MP’s office or another similar organisation • Strong political awareness and understanding of current affairs and drafting responses to policy inquiries from constituents. • Full Driving Licence • Experience of Databases and data analysis • Previous diary management experience Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 24 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will be on 5th August, start date asap afterwards Application Details Please email your CV (maximum length two pages) and a one-page cover letter outlining why you want the role and why you would be a suitable candidate. Please email your application to Tim Davies, Chief of Staff, email tim.davies@parliament.uk If you would like an informal chat about this role please phone the constituency office and speak to Tim 01453621304 Website : http://www.simonopher.org

item.title: Account Executive (Health)
item.description: WA Communications is looking for a new Account Executive to join our industry-leading, award-winning health team recently named a Communique consultancy of the year for a fifth successive time. This is a fantastic opportunity for an aspiring public affairs and corporate communciations consultant who is keen to learn, make a difference and get ahead in their agency career. You’ll get involved across our health client base in life sciences, medtech and the charity sector, broaden your communications skills and gain experience in business development. You’ll play your part in our growing team, solving client problems, delivering creative and purposeful campaigns, and producing audience research and insights to shape communications programmes. You’ll expand your expertise across a range of channels, including media relations and social comms, driving communications and engagement to reach a variety of influential stakeholders, including journalists, commentators, patients, policymakers and healthcare professionals. There’ll be excellent professional development support to help you develop and grow the skills to move forward in your career. For further details about the role and WA, please see: https://wacomms.com/account-executive-health/ Closing Date : 20 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates We may start interviewing strong candidates who apply early, so you are encouraged to apply soon. Your cover letter should not be longer than one page, setting out your interest in the role and why you believe you are a good choice for it. item.title: Community Network Manager
item.description: Job purpose The Community Network Manager will establish and lead PLP's Community Knowledge Exchange — a new network bringing together people and organisations with lived experience of marginalisation, frontline partners, lawyers, researchers and others to share knowledge and shape fairer systems. At the heart of the role is a commitment to centring lived experience in PLP's work in ways that are ethical, equitable and non-extractive. The post holder will translate community insight into policy influence, strategic litigation and advocacy, while also strengthening PLP's internal capacity to engage in co-production and lived experience partnership working effectively. They will develop and help to steward PLP's relationship with The National Lottery Community Fund and engage with the wider cohort of funded organisations, identifying opportunities for collaboration and collective endeavour. Internally, they will act as a champion and adviser, developing tools, guidance and approaches that help colleagues across PLP engage meaningfully with communities and frontline partners. Closing Date : 26 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Closing time for applications 11.59pm Initial Interviews will be held online on Thursday 6 and Friday 7 August 2026. Final interviews will be held on Friday 14 August 2026, ideally in person . Application Details For more details about the role, job description, person specs and application, please navigate to: https://publiclawproject.org.uk/latest/were-hiring-community-network-manager/ Website : http://publiclawproject.org.uk/

item.title: Senior Governance Consultant & Project Officer – Centre for Governance
item.description: CfGS is a small national charity and social purpose consultancy working to strengthen governance, scrutiny and democratic accountability across local government and the public sector. We're recruiting for two full-time, permanent, home-based roles: Senior Governance Consultant – Band 3, up to £52,000 A chance to shape governance and scrutiny excellence across local government in England. You'll design and deliver governance and scrutiny support with councils and partners, bringing both relationship and rigour to complex political environments. Suited to an experienced local government, governance or scrutiny practitioner ready to work at a national level. Project Officer – Band 2, up to £35,000 A great way into democracy, local government and public service improvement. You'll help deliver, develop and communicate CfGS's consultancy, learning and thought leadership work – ideal for someone early career, or bringing relevant experience from local government, politics, a charity, policy, research or communications. Closing Date : 3 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates • Application deadline: 9am • Shortlisting completed: 7 August 2026 • Interviews (online): week commencing 14 August 2026, flexible on exact date/time. Application Details Please send your CV, a brief summary of your relevant skills and experience, and a short statement telling us why you are interested in the role, to info@cfgs.org.uk , marking the email with the role title ("Senior Governance Consultant" or "Project Officer") Before applying please read the full job descriptions on our website at the following links: https://www.cfgs.org.uk/new-opportunity-at-cfgs-senior-governance-consultant/ and https://www.cfgs.org.uk/new-opportunity-at-cfgs-project-officer/. item.title: Senior Parliamentary Assistant
item.description: Alex Ballinger MP is recruiting a Senior Parliamentary Assistant for his Westminster office. The role requires a strong understanding of parliamentary processes and previous experience working in or closely with Parliament. The post holder will provide senior strategic, research and operational support, ensuring the MP is fully informed and briefed on parliamentary, policy and constituency matters. Key duties include conducting and overseeing research, analysing complex information, preparing high-quality briefing notes, reports, parliamentary questions, media content and policy advice, and monitoring legislation, political developments and media coverage. The successful candidate will manage complex casework and constituent enquiries, handle correspondence and complaints on the MP’s behalf, and take ownership of policy-related casework. They will be responsible for horizon-scanning emerging issues, identifying risks and opportunities, and developing research and project strategies to support the MP’s objectives. The Senior Parliamentary Assistant will be involved in the MP’s external communications including drafting press materials, monitoring and responding to social media activity, publicising parliamentary work, and supporting the office. Other responsibilities include diary management, responding to enquiries from constituents, stakeholders and interest groups, progressing casework, supporting events, and working outside normal hours when necessary. The role requires strong organisational, analytical, leadership and communication skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities while providing high-quality advice and support to the MP. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 2 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will take place at the beginning of August in London. Application Details Applications should be made by sending a CV and cover letter to matthew.collins@parliament.uk Website : http://alexballinger.org.uk
item.dc:creator: Alex Ballinger MP (Halesowen)

item.title: Research Support Officer
item.description: CALL FOR APPLICATIONS (Call ID: 95/2026) Posts of Full-Time Research Support Officer II Funded by the Research Cluster INSIGHTS (Intelligence and Security Research Cluster) of the Faculty of Media and Knowledge Sciences, University of Malta The posts are related to all projects undertaken by the Research Cluster INSIGHTS. 1. Applications are invited for Full-Time Research Support Officers (RSOs) to carry out duties in relation to the Research Cluster INSIGHTS of the Faculty of Media and Knowledge Sciences, University of Malta and all the projects associated with it. 2. Applicants must be in possession of a Master’s degree in a discipline related to humanities or in an area of study related to the topic of the Research Cluster. Applicants are required to demonstrate excellent speaking and writing skills in English. Familiarity with the local context and institutions will be considered favourably. The University of Malta is an Equal Opportunity employer . 3. The selected candidates must be living in Malta for the period of employment . 4. The posts are for an initial period of 12 months, which may be extended up to 36 months, subject to the availability of the funds. The successful candidates will be expected to start on 1st October 2026 and the initial remuneration per annum (inclusive of any cost-of-living adjustments) shall be €30,327. 5. The successful candidates will be required to enrol in a full-time PhD related to the project with the University of Malta within the first six months of employment. The project shall be related to one of the Research Cluster’s areas of research in: Pillar 1 – Human-Centric Security : This pillar seeks PhDs on issues related to, but not limited to: social change, migration and racism, trafficking, gender-based violence, climate change and (preferably) their intersection with both legacy and new forms of media and communications, as the bedrock of functioning democracies and secure societies. Pillar 2 – Technological innovation & cybersecurity : This pillar seeks PhDs on issues related to, but not limited to: forensic linguistics, security technologies, cybercrime, OSINT and digital investigations. The candidate would be required to take an inter and multi-disciplinary research approach blending technological knowledge with human rights. 6. Candidates must upload their covering letter, curriculum vitae, and certificates (certificates should be submitted in English) and contact details of at least two referees through this form here by not later than Monday, 7th September 2026 . In their covering letter, applicants shall include a short description (300 to 500 words) of their planned area of research for their PhD studies, outlining how this is related and relevant to the Research Cluster’s theme. Late applications will not be considered . 7. Further information may be obtained from our career site and should you have any queries, please send us an email on projects.hrmd@um.edu.mt . Further Information 1. The Research Cluster’s area of research aspires to become the leading research cluster in the field of intelligence and security research in the Mediterranean, building bridges across multiple disciplines, public and private stakeholders, as well as security practice and academic scholarship. Security challenges are multi-dimensional and crosscutting, ranging from the more ‘traditional’ forms— such as conflict and war, terrorism and organised crime—to ‘new’ or more ‘non-traditional’ forms— including gender violence, human smuggling, climate change, disinformation and hybrid warfare. The multi-dimensional nature of security and insecurity demands the multi-disciplinary perspective that INSIGHTS seek to provide. INSIGHTS wishes to build on its members’ current track record of internationally - acknowledged research embracing issues and partners on all continents. We believe that by harnessing the collective wisdom and real-world experiences of a diverse academic community covering an extensive span of subjects from artificial intelligence to intelligence studies, law, education, political science, international relations and gender studies we gain invaluable insights into the ever-changing threat landscape. This allows us to prioritise and address the most critical risks, to individuals, organisations and states alike, empowering actors to make informed decisions and optimise resource allocation. Further information may be obtained by contacting Dr Aitana Radu at aitana.radu@um.edu.mt . 2. The appointee will be responsible for carrying out research and offering administrative support, with their main duties including: Enrol in a full-time PhD related to the Research Cluster’s area of research; Carry out administrative duties related to the Research Cluster and its associated projects (e.g. minute taking, organisation of meetings, seminars, workshops, conferences and outreach activities, acquisition raising, report writing, etc.); Participate in the Cluster’s activities as the need arises (e.g. academic conferences, public outreach activities, scientific manuscript writing, etc.); Administer and maintain the Research Cluster’s website; Assist the other Cluster RSOs and members in applying for further project funding and research grants. Keep detailed progress reports and abide to all the conditions imposed by the Cluster and its projects; and Perform any other tasks related to the Cluster as instructed by the Research Cluster Chair(s) and Executive Committee. 3. The appointee will be expected to work at such places and during such hours as may be determined by the University authorities. 4. The selection procedure will involve: scrutiny of qualifications, research plan and experience claimed and supported by testimonials and/or certificates (copies to be included with the application); shortlisting; and an interview and / or extended interview. 5. The posts are for an initial period of 12 months, which will be subject to a probationary period and to the provisions of the Statutes, Regulations and Bye-Laws of the University of Malta which are now or which may hereafter be in force.
item.dc:creator: University of Malta

item.title: Postdoctoral Research Associate in Applied Machine Learning for High-Stakes Regulated Domains
item.description: Salary is £ 41,374 - £48,639 per annum (including London Allowance)* * This is the expected starting salary for this post however appointment at a higher point may be made for candidates who demonstrate exceptional skills and experience relevant to the role. Applications are invited for the post of Postdoctoral Research Associate in Applied Machine Learning for High-Stakes Regulated Domains in the Department of Computer Science at Royal Holloway, University of London. This is a full-time, fixed-term post for one year from September 2026, with some flexibility around the start date. The project seeks to advance the development and application of reliable, interpretable and uncertainty-aware machine learning methods for high-stakes regulated domains, including law, finance, policy and regulatory decision-making. These are settings in which errors may have significant legal, financial or social consequences, and where machine learning systems must be robust, transparent and trustworthy. The post holder will contribute to research on applied machine learning methods that support robust decision-making under uncertainty. This may involve working with structured datasets, panel data, text data, regulatory documents, legal materials, financial information and policy reports. The role will include designing and evaluating machine learning models, developing experimental studies, analysing research findings, and contributing to academic publications and wider dissemination. The successful candidate will have a PhD, or be near completion of a PhD in a discipline relevant to high-stakes regulated domains, such as regulation, finance, public policy, governance, or socio-legal studies. They should have a strong interest in applying machine learning and data-driven methods to legal, financial, policy or regulatory decision-making. They will have strong knowledge of machine learning research methods, excellent programming skills, and the ability to communicate complex ideas clearly to academic and non-specialist audiences. Experience in trustworthy AI, uncertainty quantification, interpretability, robustness, high-stakes decision-making, law, finance or regulation would be highly desirable. The role offers an excellent opportunity to contribute to an emerging and important area of applied machine learning research, with potential impact across sectors where AI systems must be reliable, accountable and safe. The post holder will be expected to conduct both independent and collaborative research, develop and evaluate machine learning methods, contribute to publications in leading academic venues, identify new research directions, and support the wider goals of the project. The successful candidate will join a vibrant and supportive research environment within the Department of Computer Science at Royal Holloway, University of London, and will contribute to the growing area of trustworthy AI and machine learning for high-stakes regulated domains. In return, we offer a highly competitive rewards and benefits package, including: Generous annual leave entitlement Rich training and development programmes A pension scheme with generous employer contribution Various schemes, including Cycle to Work, Season Ticket Loans and help with the cost of eyesight testing Free parking The post is based in Egham, Surrey, where the University is situated on a beautiful, leafy campus near Windsor Great Park and within commuting distance of London. For an informal discussion about the post, please contact: Dr Khuong An Nguyen at Khuong.Nguyen@rhul.ac.uk . For queries about the application process, please contact: Human Resources Department by email at recruitment@rhul.ac.uk . Please quote reference: 0726-230 Closing Date: 23:59, 22 July 2026 Interview Date: To be confirmed
item.dc:creator: Royal Holloway, University of London
